title: #vendors: 1819001234
preview:
Priya Rao: Heads up  QuotaPilot sent our renewal with a new 'burst credits' SKU and bumped per-embedding rates. Looks different from last year.
Ben Carter: can you paste the lines?
Priya Rao: pasted:
```
QuotaPilot Renewal 2026
- Enterprise base: $20,000/mo
- Burst credits (new): $3,750/mo
- Embedding unit: $0.003 per embed (was $0.002)
- No renewal discount quoted
```
Maya Liu: We had a 12m commit last year with 8% renewal discount.
Priya Rao: contract QP-RED-2025 confirms $216k commit, 8% off, net $198,720.
Jonah Park: @billing-bot attach last invoice and contract, and pull month-by-month embedding counts for last 6 months.
billing-bot: Attached: contract_QP-RED-2025.pdf, invoice_2025-12.pdf. Embedding counts (last 6 months) queued to finance.
Ben Carter: Drafting email: ask for line-item justification for burst credits, usage tieout, reinstated discount or multi-year pricing, and a 30-day pricing hold. Do not auto-approve addon.
Maya Liu: If they insist, request a pilot (3mo free) or 25% discount on addon.
Priya Rao: agree. Ben, send and cc procurement@redwood. I'll monitor.

title: #eng-ml: 1811112222
preview:
Alice Nguyen: Heads-up — did an ember-liteq int8 canary this morning, and we're seeing a small but consistent class of funcall failures (parsing errors) on multi-tool flows.
Ben Carter: Ugh, can you paste an example log?
Alice Nguyen: Pasted sample below — pattern is trailing partial NDJSON / orphaned `,` fragments attached after the funcall payload. Only shows up when streaming is enabled + small batch sizes.
Alice Nguyen: ```
{"tool":"search","args":{...}}
,
{"tool":"open_url","args":{...}}
````
Carlos Ruiz: So the model outputs an extra comma + mini-object after the expected JSON? That's weird. Any chance it's streaming boundary cut?
Alice Nguyen: Exactly. It looks like the streaming chunk boundary sometimes delivers an extra chunk that contains a leftover `,
{` snippet. Parsers then treat the stream as ndjson and choke.
Dana Li: @Alice can you share the canary config (batch, stream, kvcache) and the exact model tag?
Alice Nguyen: ember-liteq:v2-int8, batch=4, stream=true, kv_cache=enabled, prefix_cache=off. Canary was mirrored against raven-v2 baseline (no failure). Failure rate ~0.62% over 10k requests.
Ben Carter: Baseline no-fail suggests model generation differences (tokenization offsets?). Could be a tokenizer change or lingering EOS behavior.
Carlos Ruiz: If it's only on streaming + small batches that points at chunking logic in runtime + model emitting non-atomic JSON fragments. Could be beam/decoder scheduling.
Alice Nguyen: I also grabbed a raw token trace from the runtime: shows tokens that correspond to `,` and partial `{ \"id\":` emitted after a chunk boundary.
Alice Nguyen: ```
... [TOK_123]=" ," [TOK_124]="
" [TOK_125]="{" [TOK_126]="\"id\"" ...
```
Dana Li: Two quick mitigation / investigation tracks: 1) Gatekeeper pre-parse coercion — tolerant JSON recovery (strip trailing commas, close open braces) as a stopgap for canary. 2) Repro harness — fuzz streaming chunk splits to reproduce.
Ben Carter: For 1) we should be careful — coercion can mask real regressions. I'd prefer gatekeeper to have an opt-in permissive parsing mode per-route with explicit telemetry flag.
Carlos Ruiz: Agreed. But for customer-facing routes we can enable permissive parsing only in Dedicated/dev environments. Add a metric for coerced parses so we can see how often this happens.
Alice Nguyen: Proposal: add a new gatekeeper config: `structured_output.coercion: [\"strip_trailing_commas\",\"append_closing_brace\"]` and `telemetry.coercion_tag=true`. Default off.
Ben Carter: Sounds good. Can you sketch a quick implementation plan + PR? I'll take the gatekeeper change if you want.
Alice Nguyen: I'll open a repro harness issue + attach sample tokens. Ben — if you can own the gatekeeper PR, I'll send the canary replay with varied chunk sizes.
Dana Li: I'll add an automated fuzz job to the onb-board harness to vary stream chunking, sampling across batch sizes 1..8.
Carlos Ruiz: Long-term: update model onboarding checklist to run streaming-chunk fuzz and diff tokenized outputs vs baseline. Add a step to record token boundary distributions.
Alice Nguyen: Action items: 
- Alice: repro harness + sample token traces (done by EOD)
- Ben: gatekeeper permissive parse PR + telemetry (assign)
- Dana: fuzz job + dashboard slice (assign)
- Carlos: update onboarding checklist and docs.
Alice Nguyen: Any objections to enabling permissive parsing on the ember-liteq canary in staging for 24h to collect coercion metrics? :hourglass_flowing_sand:
Ben Carter: fine with me as long as telemetry is on and we revert after 24h.
Carlos Ruiz: +1
Dana Li: +1, but add a post-run blameless writeup.
eval-bot: [automated] recorded canary run 1811112222: 10000 requests, parse_fail_rate=0.0062, coercion_enabled=false.
Alice Nguyen: Great — will proceed with repro + PRs. If we see coercion rate >0.5% on staging we'll hold the model.
Ben Carter: Thx — ping me when PR is up and I'll review.
Carlos Ruiz: Thanks all.
Dana Li: Thanks :thumbsup:

title: #devex: 1805123456
preview:
Sam Park: hey team — working on my first PR into main. can someone quick me on the expected flow, branch naming, and any style/commit rules? also what CI should I expect to see? :pray:

Kevin Li: welcome Sam! TL;DR: branch from main, keep PRs small, run local lint/tests, request 1 reviewer from your area. infra/runtime changes need extra reviewers. :wave:

Olivia Chen: quick checklist I send new folks: 1) branch name: feature/<JIRA>-short-desc or fix/<JIRA>-short-desc (e.g. feature/ABC-123-add-healthcheck). 2) commit message header: <scope>: <short summary> (50 chars max). 3) run pre-commit and ruff/black. 4) include a short PR description + test plan. 5) add labels (area/runtime/docs).

Olivia Chen: sample commit and branch:
```
git checkout -b feature/ABC-123-add-telemetry
git commit -m "telemetry: add request duration metric for inference path"
```

Ben Carter: CI you'll see: lint (ruff), unit tests (pytest), mypy for typed modules, and a smoke e2e on the dedicated pipeline for infra-affecting changes. If any check fails GitHub actions will post logs in the PR. 

Ben Carter: policy: small features => squash-and-merge. Larger refactors keep separate commits and @kevin for approval. If you touch serving/runtime, tag @eng-runtime or @eng-infra depending on scope.

Sam Park: thanks — how do I run the tests locally quickly? some of the CI jobs are long.

Kevin Li: for most changes run unit tests only:
```
# from repo root
make venv && source .venv/bin/activate
poetry run pytest tests/unit -q
```
For the full stack smoke (slower) use the docker compose helper:
```
docker-compose -f docker/dev.yml run --rm web pytest tests/smoke -q
```
Local lint/format: `pre-commit run --all-files` (should auto-fix formatting).

Olivia Chen: small tip: put integration or heavy tests behind markers so they don't run on every local dev run. See tests/README.md for markers. Also add a short `changes:` section in the PR description if user-visible.

onboard-bot: FYI — please review the PR checklist in #devex-docs before requesting reviews: https://redwood.example/docs/pr-checklist :robot_face:

Sam Park: perfect, thanks everyone — I'll push a branch and tag reviewers when ready. :thumbsup:

Kevin Li: ping @Ben Carter or me if CI is flaky. and welcome again!

Ben Carter: also drop a note in #new-hires with a link to your PR if you want extra eyes.

title: #customer-success: 1798765432
preview:
Priya Rao: Heads up — KestrelAI activity cratered this week, ~64% drop in DAUs. Their Head of Data (Marta) left Friday and procurement told our AE they're running a 2-week proof with CloudX. Need a quick risk play.
Tom Alvarez: Oof. do we have seat-level churn & which teams paused?
Priya Rao: short summary — 420 active seats last week -> 150 now. Top 3 product teams paused. Advocate was Marta (Head of Data). New contact is an interim PM, not responsive yet.
Evan Kim: drop the raw numbers and i'll pull infra logs to see if it's us (errors/timeouts) or client-side patterns.
Priya Rao: paste of daily active seats (last 7 days):
```
2026-03-14: 420
2026-03-15: 380
2026-03-16: 290
2026-03-17: 240
2026-03-18: 180
2026-03-19: 160
2026-03-20: 150
```
Priya Rao: also flagged by support — increased model response time for long prompts (timeouts) during 3/16–3/18 window. might've triggered the pilot.
Janelle Ortiz: noted. i saw 5 error SLO alerts for kestrel-prod in that window (e-4321, e-4322). i've linked support case #9876 to the account and added initial notes.
Ben Carter: propose short mitigation: 1) offer one-week free Dedicated slot to remove perf pain, 2) run a quick joint postmortem and proactive QBR with the interim PM, 3) prep a competitive brief vs CloudX highlighting latency/cost and model parity.
Tom Alvarez: +1. i'll own the CloudX pricing/latency comp. also recommend a canary rollback policy if we spin up Dedicated for them.
Evan Kim: i'll do perf root cause. if it's infra/kernel/runtime -> push hotfix asap. if it's user prompt patterns -> create enablement doc + quick prompt templates and a short co-run session.
Priya Rao: i'm reaching out to the interim PM now with proposed times for a 30m sync and offering the Dedicated pilot + a week of co-run enablement. will paste outreach copy in-thread.
Tom Alvarez: ping me the outreach copy and i'll review. also flag for AM exec escalation if no reply in 48h.
alert-bot: usage anomaly detected for account=kestrelai, drop_percent=64, started=2026-03-16T03:12:00Z
Priya Rao: capturing next steps: 1) Evan triage (perf vs config) due EOD, 2) Tom competitive brief due +1 day, 3) Priya outreach & Dedicated offer now, 4) Janelle to keep support case warm and surface any new errors. i'll schedule follow-up in #customer-success tomorrow.
Ben Carter: good plan. escalate to AM execs if no response within 48h.
Priya Rao: agreed — will escalate if no reply by 2026-03-22 noon PST.

title: #all-hands: 1798001111
preview:
Ruth Kim: Quick thread — live all-hands in 20. Can AV confirm backup plan for video blackouts?
Liam (AV): Bring 2 spare HDMI + DP cables. If black screen, switch to slides-only scene and keep audio live.
Ben Carter: Can we move roadmap Q&A to first slot? We've got execs late.
Sophie - Comms: Prefer Q&A at end for audience engagement. Compromise: brief live poll during roadmap, full Q&A still at end.
Anya - Product: Linking a short poll for prioritizing questions would help. I'll pin it.
StreamBot: :warning: Ingest endpoint us-east-2 showed 250ms spike 10m ago. Monitoring.
Liam (AV): If ingest degrades, encoder fallback command: sed -i 's/ingest-us-east/ingest-us-east-back/' /etc/stream && systemctl restart stream
Ben Carter: Note for presenters — disable screen savers and set display to "never sleep".
Sophie - Comms: Deck will be uploaded to shared drive immediately after; transcript aims to be up within 90 minutes.
Ruth Kim: Action items: AV bring spares, Product prepare 2 extra roadmap slides, Comms will handle post-meeting assets.
Liam (AV): Doing a quick mic sweep now. Audio peaks at -5dB, lowering compression.
StreamBot: :white_check_mark: Recording scheduled at 17:00 UTC.
Ruth Kim: Thanks all — see you in the green room in 15. Drop any last-minute questions here.

title: #eng-oncall: 1785012345
preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: PagerDuty alert fired (PD-82419) — p99 latency > 2.5s for model-serving-prod in eu-west-1. Alert rule: p99_latency_route > 2.5s for 3m. Auto-page to eng-oncall.
PagerDuty: Incident PD-82419 created and routed to Priya Nair (on-call).
Priya Nair: acked on PD, jumping in. will check recent deploys + routing.
Jon Becker: on it — looking at autoscaler + queue backlog. seeing CPU stable but GPU queue length up.
Sasha Liu: checking KV cache eviction logs and hotkeys. quick grep shows many evictions around the spike.
Priya Nair: timeline — spike started ~1785012280 per metrics, coincides with a canary rollout of model v2.3.0 about 6m ago.
Jon Becker: suspects rollout + batch config mismatch causing tail latency. reverting canary?
Priya Nair: not yet — let's try mitigation first: scale dedicated pool and reduce max_batch_size for v2.3.0. If that fails, rollback.
OpsBot: runbook link added: https://redwood/runbooks/latency-tail-spike — checklist appended to thread.
Sasha Liu: snippet from kvcache logs:
```
2026-04-05T12:10:11Z WARN kvcache eviction key=customer:hotkey:12345 size=482MB
2026-04-05T12:10:12Z INFO kvcache miss key=customer:hotkey:12345
2026-04-05T12:10:13Z ERROR serve latency_ms=2791 req_id=abcd1234
```
Jon Becker: running command to bump capacity in eu-west-1: `infractl pool resize --pool model-serving-prod-eu --add 4-gpu`
Priya Nair: doing a quick config patch to force max_batch_size=8 for v2.3.0 in canary. patch applied to 2 nodes. monitoring.
PagerDuty: PD-82419 acknowledged by Priya Nair. Escalation paused.
Sasha Liu: checking customer-facing impact — dashboards show 0.8% of requests above 2.5s in last 5m, concentrated on a small set of API keys (hotkey pattern).
Ben Carter: support here — if it's only a subset, we can route those keys to fallback model variant until we stabilize. drafting message to affected accounts.
Priya Nair: agreed, Sasha, can you publish a temporary routing rule? e.g., `route.override --key-prefix customer:hotkey --model model-v2.2.6`
Sasha Liu: done. rule applied and propagated to edge within ~45s.
incident-bot: autoscale action completed — 4 GPUs added to model-serving-prod-eu.
Jon Becker: after the scale + batch cap + hotkey reroute, p99 dropped to ~1.1s. still watching for any oscillation.
Priya Nair: can everyone pls add short reaction when they confirm their task? I want a clear handoff once stable. :white_check_mark:
Sasha Liu: confirmed — kvcache eviction rate down, hotkey reroute holding. :white_check_mark:
Jon Becker: confirmed — autoscaler scaled, queue drained. :white_check_mark:
Ben Carter: support note queued, will send once we hit 10m stable window.
Priya Nair: plan: keep increased capacity + hotkey reroute for 30m, then either lift or rollback canary based on stability. If p99 reverts >1.8s we'll rollback v2.3.0.
incident-bot: incident updated: PD-82419 state=acknowledged, priority=high, runbook=latency-tail-spike.
Priya Nair: quick postmortem items to capture in thread: (1) canary batch config mismatch; (2) lack of hotkey pre-check in rollout; (3) consider autoscaler faster scale-up policy for tail events.
Jon Becker: adding metric alert to surface hotkey evictions earlier.
Ben Carter: will prepare customer-facing summary and share draft here before send.
Priya Nair: handing off to on-call rotation next in 15m. if stable at handoff, handoff note: PD-82419 - mitigated, monitoring.
OpsBot: handoff checklist ticked by Priya Nair.
Priya Nair: nice work team. closing communication channel in 20m unless we see regression. :pray:

Ben Carter: support here — if it's only a subset, we can route those keys to fallback model variant until we stabilize. drafting message to affected accounts.

incident-bot: incident updated: PD-82419 state=acknowledged, priority=high, runbook=latency-tail-spike.
Priya Nair: quick postmortem items to capture in thread: (1) canary batch config mismatch; (2) lack of hotkey pre-check in rollout; (3) consider autoscaler faster scale-up policy for tail events.
Jon Becker: adding metric alert to surface hotkey evictions earlier.
Ben Carter: will prepare customer-facing summary and share draft here before send.
Priya Nair: handing off to on-call rotation next in 15m. if stable at handoff, handoff note: PD-82419 - mitigated, monitoring.
OpsBot: handoff checklist ticked by Priya Nair.
Priya Nair: nice work team. closing communication channel in 20m unless we see regression. :pray:

title: #eng-security: 1780123456
preview:
Alice Chen: heads-up, vendor published GPU stack advisories (driver + cuDNN). initial CVEs called out: CVE-2026-0421 (driver privilege escalation) and CVE-2026-0422 (cuDNN malformed tensor OOB). NCCL had a separate infoleak fix in 2.17.0 (CVE-2026-0550).
Marco Diaz: paste the link? need to map affected builds and our images.
Alice Chen: https://vendor.example/nvidia/advisory/2026-0421-0422 - drivers 535.86+ and cuDNN 9.2.x affected per vendor.
Priya Nair: TL;DR: high local exploitability. For hosted multitenant nodes this is HIGH (processes run as root on GPU nodes). Dedicated/private less urgent but still P1 for compliance.
Ben Carter: prioritization suggestion: Hosted: 48h emergency patch (canary -> stage -> prod). Dedicated: 30-day advisory + scheduling. Private: publish patch bundles + compatibility notes.
sec-oncall: Also ensure audit logging captures driver/package installs and package sigs. Add KMS signature verification into the deployment flow.
Alice Chen: Agreed. Action item: add post-install smoke to capture signature/hash into audit S3. Example: `rpm -qa --qf "%{NAME} %{VERSION} %{SIGPGP:pgpsig}
"` and push to s3-audit immutable bucket.
Marco Diaz: compatibility risk: some quantized kernels compiled against older cuDNN ABI. We saw regressions on llm-inference-2.1 benchmarks last update. Need matrix: model-family x cuda/cuDNN x quant-profile.
Priya Nair: I can kick off matrix jobs. Quick QA smoke command for infra:
```
./smoke-test.sh --model gpt-2-onnx --cuda 12.1 --cudnn 9.2 --seed 42 --batch 1
```
Add variants: `--quant=4bit` and `--kvcache`.
infra-bot: Canary cluster deploy scheduled: canary-ggp-1 -> 2026-03-21T02:00Z. Rolling window 2h. Notifications enabled.
Ben Carter: owners/timeline (proposed):
- Priya: triage + full smoke matrix (6h)
- Infra: canary patch & infra smoke (12h)
- Marco: rollback validation & compat notes (24h)
- Alice/Ben: customer comms + Dedicated advisory (36h)
Alice Chen: RBAC/audit question: restrict who can trigger kernel/driver upgrades? recommend temporary `driver-upgrade` role scoped to infra-oncall + security.
sec-oncall: +1. For private tenant nodes require dual-approval and log approvals. Keep approvals in audit trail.
Priya Nair: Early matrix results: cuDNN 9.2.1 reduces the OOB behavior but introduced 3-6% p95 latency for small-seq quantized models. Perf cap: <10% regression acceptable, but evaluate cost delta.
Marco Diaz: tradeoff plan: if latency regression >10% on any high-QPS route we fallback those routes to a pinned runtime_variant with older cuDNN. We have policy routing by `runtime_variant` tag. Can we route 5% traffic to canary for 6h?
Alice Chen: yes, add canary tag `gpu_patch_canary=true` and set route to 5% for initial validation.
Ben Carter: KMS signing follow-up: vendor-signed packages exist, but we want to re-sign our internal distro. Who owns keys? Ops or Security?
sec-oncall: Security owns keys. We'll provision a signing key in HSM/KMS and grant infra temporary SignGrant for the rollout jobs. Document procedure under security/kms-driver-signing.
Priya Nair: test checklist to run across matrix:
- startup smoke (drivers load)
- forward/backward correctness with kv-cache
- NCCL multi-process allreduce sanity
- perf microbench (p50/p95)
- golden-token E2E check (token diffs)
Marco Diaz: add embedding similarity threshold and a small golden-token regression check. If quality drifts we block.
Alice Chen: draft customer advisory: short statement + mitigations (isolate GPU nodes, disable multi-tenant scheduling, enforce RBAC). I'll draft and loop in legal/security for disclosure timing.
Ben Carter: rollback playbook sketch: revert driver rpm, restore module deps, clear nvpeer caches, restart runtime. Marco to validate in staging.
Marco Diaz: validating rollback now in staging. will post results. If blocking regressions found we will hold canary and escalate.
infra-bot: Canary deploy completed. 5% traffic routed. Monitoring hooks enabled: latency, error rate, OOMs, security-audit events.
Priya Nair: canary initial: no errors except one route with quantized models showing 6% p95 increase. investigating model-specific kernel path.
sec-oncall: label audit logs with `gpu-patch-2026-0421` for easier forensics. Ensure logs are immutable for 90 days.
Alice Chen: summary/actions:
- Priya: finish matrix + perf QA (6h)
- Marco: complete rollback validation + compat report (12h)
- Ben: finalize customer advisory + Dedicated customer scheduling (36h)
- Security: KMS signing policy + audit retention config (24h)
I'll update thread when comms ready. Thanks all :thumbsup:

title: #people-ops: 1780126789
preview:
Carlos Mendez: Hey team - accepted the offer, super excited. Quick immigration Q: I'm currently on OPT and my employer filed an H-1B. My school portal shows cap-gap extension, but I haven't received any receipt yet. Can I still start on the proposed Oct 2 start date? Also how does this affect benefits eligibility and I-9?

Sofia Martinez: Congrats Carlos! Were excited too. We can't give legal advice but we'll coordinate with our immigration counsel (Priya) to confirm. Operationally: we require a completed I-9 before payroll runs. Remote/authorized-rep I-9 is possible if you can't come onsite before your first paycheck. Please upload any passport, I-94, EAD (if any), SSN, and any H-1B receipt/notice to the secure HR portal and mark them private.

Ben Carter: Hiring manager here - team is OK with a remote-first start and flexible onboarding. If paperwork is delayed we can push new-hire training by a week. Keep us posted.

Derek Li: Payroll heads-up: cutoff for Oct payroll is Sep 25. If I-9 isn't complete before payroll processing we may not be able to pay on the first cycle; we will do our best to expedite but need docs in the system. Benefits enrollment window opens on your hire packet acceptance and must be completed within 30 days. Benefits effective date may be the 1st of the next month depending on plan rules. We'll flag your record once I-9 is done so benefits team can advise exact effective date.

Priya Rao: Hi Carlos quick note from counsel: if you have a cap-gap extension reflected in your SEVIS or your DSO has authorized cap-gap, that can extend your F-1 status/OPT work authorization under certain conditions. Please share any proof (DSO email, SEVIS stamp, or an I-797 receipt if you get it). We'll review and advise whether that will satisfy I-9 requirements for employment authorization. If you don't have proof in time, we can discuss start-date flexibility or a short contractor arrangement, but any contractor option requires legal and procurement signoff.

Carlos Mendez: Thanks. I uploaded passport.pdf, i94.png, opt_ead.jpg to the portal. No I-797 yet. My DSO emailed a cap-gap note - I put that in too. Is the DSO email OK for now?

Sofia Martinez: Got the uploads, thanks. We'll wait for Priya to confirm. For I-9, in many cases a receipt or DSO confirmation can be used temporarily while counsel reviews, but we'll log a follow-up requirement to present the final document once available. PeopleOpsBot: reminder - do not paste PII in this channel; use the secure portal for documents.

Priya Rao: I reviewed the DSO note you uploaded. It helps but we'll need either an official SEVIS cap-gap annotation or a USCIS receipt notice to finalize counsel advice. I'll email you privately with the exact docs we can accept for I-9 verification and any timing risks for travel or work authorization.

Derek Li: Payroll: once Sofia marks the I-9 complete well tag the employee record and run onboarding payroll checks. If the I-9 is pending we can set a manual payroll hold but that requires sign-off. Carlos, please confirm if you want us to hold pay until docs are final or try to process with a follow-up requirement.

Carlos Mendez: I'd prefer we try to process and Ill get any missing docs ASAP. If it becomes a problem, we can accept a one-pay-delay. Appreciate the flexibility.

Sofia Martinez: Noted. We'll attempt to process with a follow-up requirement. Priya: please DM next steps. Ben: I'll add a calendar note to postpone any instructor-led onboarding if paperwork delays beyond 1 week.

PeopleOpsBot: reminder_sent: Secure upload required for immigration documents. Public channels are not approved for PII.

Carlos Mendez: Thank you all - will watch for Priya's DM and update the portal when I get the receipt.

Ben Carter: Welcome aboard, Carlos. Catch you in orientation.

Sofia Martinez: Loop closure: documents received, counsel engaged, payroll aware, hiring manager flexible. Reopen thread if anything changes.

title: #customer-success: 1778884444
preview:
Diego Morales: flag from renewal check-ins: three customers mentioned the same UX friction around model selection. feels like a cross-account signal. wondering if we should standardize how we surface multi-account recurring asks.
Leah Chen: agreed. PMs miss the pattern when items are scattered. what if CS created a tiny "Lighthouse Card" we can pin to the top of the notes so PMs see the pattern immediately?
Priya Singh: like a lighthouse that highlights repeated pain across customers? i like the metaphor. can you propose a compact template?
Diego Morales: 2-line template suggestion below 5 paste into notes or thread reply.
```
Lighthouse Card
- Issue (1 short sentence) + sample account count
- Business effect (renewal risk / onboarding delay / revenue impact)
- Suggested short-term workaround
- Priority: P0/P1/P2
- Tag(s): onboarding | renewal-risk | integrations | ux
```
Ben Carter: can we add a tiny field: "pattern evidence" (list of 1-2 lines with customer names + quote) so PMs can see it's cross-account without digging? keeps it honest.
Diego Morales: good call. updated sample with evidence line and an example filled card.
```
Lighthouse Card
- Issue: model selection defaults hide custom models (3 accounts reported)
- Business effect: blocks onboarding / renewal risk for two pilots (~$10k ARR risk)
- Workaround: enable "show all" toggle in UI for affected orgs
- Pattern evidence: AcmeRetail: "we can't find model X"; NovaHealth: "defaults hide our model"
- Priority: P1
- Tags: onboarding | renewal-risk | ux
```
Leah Chen: love the example. can ProductOps give us a shared sheet / export so cards roll into product triage automatically?
ProductOps Bot: Created shared template: https://go.redwood.ai/cs/lighthouse-cards (editable). Rows labeled "renewal-risk" auto-export to triage board on Fridays. :robot_face:
Priya Singh: pilot plan: 2-week pilot across onboarding + renewal outreach. target: 15 cards, at least 5 with cross-account evidence. any P0 -> immediate PM escalation.
Diego Morales: i'll add "create 1 Lighthouse Card" to the renewal call checklist for this week and try to pull evidence into the sheet.
Ben Carter: RenewalBot also flagged three accounts with similar NPS comments this morning. @RenewalBot can you attach the NPS snippets to the sheet?
RenewalBot: NPS snippets for flagged accounts uploaded to the Lighthouse sheet. Recommended converts: 3 snippets -> 2 cards.
Leah Chen: great. acceptance criteria for pilot: 1) 15 cards captured in 2 weeks, 2) at least 5 show cross-account pattern evidence, 3) PM triage receives export weekly and creates at least 1 follow-up ticket if priority >= P1.
Priya Singh: i'll run a short retro at the end of pilot and recommend next steps (embed card macro in Confluence / or add to onboarding playbook).
Diego Morales: thanks team. small structure, big signal. i'll post first cards later today from AcmeRetail call.
Leah Chen: excited to see if this reduces churn surprises. :sparkles:

title: #finance: 1774506789
preview:
Aisha Noor: FYI Solstice Labs GmbH sent invoice SL-3389 for SaaS support billed to Redwood US and included 19% German VAT but DID NOT list their Umsatzsteuer-ID on the PDF. They also attached a signed W-8BEN-E. Should we hold payment? :eyes:

Ben Carter: German VAT + W-8BEN-E on the same doc feels off. For B2B cross-border services to a US legal entity, vendor normally should not charge German VAT (export of services). Also invoice must show supplier VAT ID if they charge VAT.

Lucia Romano: Agreed. W-8BEN-E is for US withholding/tax status of foreign entities and has no bearing on EU VAT. If Solstice is charging VAT they need to include their VAT number (USt-IdNr) and the basis for charging. If the supply is to a US customer, they should either zero-rate or state export of services.

Aisha Noor: I asked them to reissue invoice without VAT or provide a legal basis for charging VAT and to include their USt-IdNr. Told them we may delay payment until corrected invoice or credit note is issued.

tax-bot: Quick checks: 1) Verify vendor USt-IdNr via German BZSt (or VIES) 2) If vendor insists on charging VAT despite billing US entity, request VAT registration docs and an explanation of place-of-supply treatment.

Ben Carter: Also confirm where Solstice's billing entity is registered (DE branch vs EU holding). If they claim US source or subcontractor billing from non-EU entity, that changes treatment. But they sent a German address.

Lucia Romano: If vendor refuses to remove VAT and we pay it, we will need a credit note to reclaim on our end or treat it as non-recoverable cost depending on jurisdiction. Procurement should avoid gross-ups; if vendor asks Redwood to 'gross up' to cover their VAT, escalate to finance lead. We don't gross-up standard supplier invoices.

Solstice Labs (via email): "We are registered in DE. We charged VAT because system defaulted for EU entities. We have attached VAT registration screenshot." (attachment not yet verified)

Aisha Noor: I told them: please reissue with USt-IdNr on invoice, or else remove VAT and issue a zero-rated export invoice. Also asked them to confirm bank account matches invoicing legal entity.

payment-bot: Reminder set: follow up on SL-3389 in 4 business days.

Ben Carter: If they produce USt-IdNr, run VIES check and keep evidence. If VIES invalid, tag tax for manual review before payment.

Lucia Romano: Note for records: keep email trail and any VAT screenshots. If they insist on W-8 as proof, tell them W-8 only affects US withholding — not EU VAT.

Aisha Noor: Thanks all — putting a hold and will update once vendor replies. :white_check_mark:

Lucia Romano: If vendor refuses to remove VAT and we pay it, we will need a credit note to reclaim on our end or treat it as non-recoverable cost depending on jurisdiction. Procurement should avoid gross-ups; if vendor asks Redwood to 'gross up' to cover their VAT, escalate to finance lead. We don't gross-up standard supplier invoices.

Solstice Labs (via email): "We are registered in DE. We charged VAT because system defaulted for EU entities. We have attached VAT registration screenshot." (attachment not yet verified)

Aisha Noor: I told them: please reissue with USt-IdNr on invoice, or else remove VAT and issue a zero-rated export invoice. Also asked them to confirm bank account matches invoicing legal entity.

payment-bot: Reminder set: follow up on SL-3389 in 4 business days.

Ben Carter: If they produce USt-IdNr, run VIES check and keep evidence. If VIES invalid, tag tax for manual review before payment.

Lucia Romano: Note for records: keep email trail and any VAT screenshots. If they insist on W-8 as proof, tell them W-8 only affects US withholding — not EU VAT.

Aisha Noor: Thanks all — putting a hold and will update once vendor replies. :white_check_mark:

title: #customer-success: 1771000456

Sofia Li: Putting together the Cedar Health onboarding internal prep. Need quick roll call and who will present what. Internal sync tomorrow 10:30am PT?
Noah Brooks: +1 for 10:30. I can cover infra, perf expectations, and any capacity plumbing.
Priya Shah: I'll own success criteria and renewal watch. Quick note: Cedar's procurement window closes ~2026-06-15, so renewal risk if no production signal by day 45.
Ben Carter: I'll do quickstart + sample code. Suggest we highlight webhook consumer and python snippet in the enablement.
Sofia Li: Stakeholders to flag: Rajesh (Cedar PM) - primary stake; Marcus (security) - reviewer; Priya (Cedar data lead). Internal owners: Sofia (CS Lead), Noah (SRE/infra), Ben (SE), Priya (CS Ops).
Noah Brooks: Proposed tech timeline: Day 0 - creds & staging env; Day 1-5 - integration & schema mapping; Day 6-14 - validation + perf tuning; Day 15-30 - pilot + early roll. If they request private/VPC we need +7 days for security review.
Priya Shah: 30-day success metrics (short): 1) Staging app makes authenticated API calls; 2) >=80% of sample prompts meet quality gate; 3) Health dashboard live with alerts; 4) Rajesh confirms acceptance email.
Ben Carter: Quick code sample for the enablement slide deck to show devs a minimal consumer:
```python
# minimal consumer example
import requests
resp = requests.post('https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate', json={'model':'rwd-m','prompt':'Summarize notes'})
print(resp.json())
```
Sofia Li: Risks: procurement timeline slipping, security gating private deploy, embedding scale if they batch heavily. Renewal signal: no value in first 45 days -> high risk. Action: schedule sec touch on Day 2.
oncall-bot: Security review slot reserved for Cedar Health on 2026-04-02 14:00 UTC.
Noah Brooks: Capacity note: if Cedar expects >500 embeddings req/min recommend a short Dedicated trial or aggressive batching. Tagging @infra-sales for pricing follow-up.
Priya Shah: Action items: Ben - deck + quickstart; Noah - perf checklist + infra notes; Sofia - customer invite + pre-read; me - compile success board and post-demo survey (3 Qs). I'll capture feedback and surface renewal flags.
Sofia Li: I will run an internal dry run 30 min before customer call and then invite Rajesh and Marcus. Please add 5 example prompts to the doc: https://redwood.internal/cs/cedar-prompts (placeholder).
Ben Carter: Uploaded quickstart and "Gotchas" slide to the folder.
Sofia Li: Thanks all. Closing internal prep thread; will post customer invite link after our rehearsal. :rocket:

title: #announcements: 1769700001
preview:
Chris Osei: :mega: Marketplace Listing Refresh is LIVE (AWS + GCP)

Big thanks to everyone across PMM/Partnerships/Eng/Security/Docs/RevOps. Starting today we’re moving off the legacy marketplace listings and onto the refreshed offers + onboarding flow.

What changed (buyer-facing):
1) New marketplace plan/SKU naming (consistent across AWS + GCP)
   • Hosted API (Usage-based)
   • Dedicated Capacity (Reserved)
   • Private Deployment (VPC)
   Add-ons:
   • Compliance Pack
   • Premium Support

2) Listing content refreshed
   • Clear Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private differentiation
   • Updated screenshots + "How it works" section
   • Procurement-friendly FAQ (billing, entitlement timing, support)

3) Marketplace onboarding hardening (subscription → entitlement → org provisioning → first API key)
   • New console marketplace entrypoint
   • Entitlement verification API + improved error states
   • Entitlement sync job retries + alerting

4) Security documentation bundle for marketplace review
   • Marketplace-friendly security overview + shared responsibility
   • Data handling/retention + residency notes
   • Audit logging overview + export options

Where to find enablement (internal):
- PMM copy / positioning blocks (approved language): confluence → Sales Enablement → Positioning: "Marketplace positioning + copy guidelines"
- Procurement one-pager (when to route to marketplace vs direct): confluence → Sales Enablement → Pitch Decks: "Marketplace procurement one-pager"
- Customer-facing FAQ draft (billing, entitlements, trials/credits): drive → GTM → "customer-facing-marketplace-faq-draft"
- Security packet index (what’s public vs gated): confluence → Security & Compliance → "Marketplace security documentation pack"
- Deal ops + required HubSpot fields: drive → RevOps → "revops-process-marketplace-deal-ops"

Escalation / help:
- Provisioning/entitlement issues (stuck linking org, missing entitlement): #support (tag @Dev Patel) + open SUP ticket
- Billing dimension mismatch / SKU mapping questions: #eng-platform (tag @Cole Summers)
- Security questionnaire / evidence requests: #eng-security (tag @Naomi Feldman) + loop in @Olga Petrov if evidence gating needed

If you’re customer-facing this week: please route marketplace buyers to the new onboarding docs + FAQ first. We want to reduce “time-to-first-token” + support load.

Dropping a quick checklist in thread next.

Camila Reyes: +1 and please use the new screenshots/copy only (don’t pull from the old listing pages). If you see outdated assets floating around, ping me or @Soojin Lee.

Soojin Lee: Quick note on language: avoid saying “SOC2 compliant” in emails/decks. Use the approved phrasing from the copy guidelines (e.g., “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA”) + link the security packet index.

Jordan Blake: This is a big unlock for procurement-led deals. Thank you all. AEs: if you have an account that *must* buy via marketplace, please re-check the right plan name before you send them the subscribe link.

Alex Martinez: Docs are live + nav updated. Marketplace onboarding landing pages are now under Docs → “Marketplace”. If you hit any broken redirects or weird 404s, file INT ticket + tag me.

Elliot Price: Also: security packet publication is split (public vs gated). If a buyer asks for evidence (SOC2, pen test attestation, etc.), don’t forward random PDFs. Use the “Security packet index” page and request access via Security/GRC.

Chloe Martin: Onboarding UX notes:
- If buyer already has a Redwood org, the console now prompts to link subscription → existing org (instead of creating a duplicate)
- If entitlement hasn’t propagated yet, they’ll see a “Try again” state with guidance (and the backend retries are more aggressive now)
- If it’s been >30 min, please escalate (we have alerts but want human eyes fast)

Cole Summers: Billing/metering heads-up:
- Hosted dimensions are now buyer-friendly labels (Text generation input/output, Embeddings, Rerank)
- Dedicated includes Reserved GPU hours (+ Usage overage where applicable)
- If you see a line item that looks wrong, grab the marketplace subscription ID + Redwood org ID and tag me in #eng-platform.

Nadia Rahman: Telemetry is in place for the onboarding funnel. We’re tracking:
- entitlement lookup latency
- org linking failures
- API key creation completion
- first successful request
If you want dashboard access, ping me/Ben.

Ben Carter: Baseline conversion metrics are in the GTM drive folder ("marketplace-onboarding-funnel-baseline-metrics"). We’ll post a weekly update after 1 full week of data.

Naomi Feldman: Security escalation guardrails:
- Standard questionnaires: start with the draft response doc + cite the security packet pages
- Anything requesting raw evidence artifacts (reports, detailed diagrams, vuln scan output): loop in Security before sending
- Residency/retention questions: use the marketplace data handling + retention page (it has the current defaults + configurable options)

Dev Patel: Support macros are ready for:
- “Subscription active but no entitlement”
- “Provisioning delayed >30 minutes”
- “Billing dimension question”
We’ll pin them in #support. If an enterprise buyer is blocked during procurement/onboarding, escalate quickly — we want a clean first week.

Stephanie Nguyen: Field note: buyers will ask “what did I buy?” during org linking. The new plan names are much clearer. Please copy/paste exactly:
Hosted API (Usage-based) / Dedicated Capacity (Reserved) / Private Deployment (VPC)

Lukas Meyer: EMEA teams: Private Deployment (VPC) language is landing well with security reviewers. If anyone gets pressure for “on-prem in marketplace” specifically, flag it — we should route those to Private direct contracting.

Chris Osei: Quick internal checklist (customer-facing):
1) Confirm which marketplace (AWS vs GCP) + which plan name
2) Send: onboarding doc link + FAQ + (if needed) security packet index
3) If provisioning is stuck: collect subscription ID + Redwood org ID + timestamp, open SUP ticket, tag @Dev Patel
4) If pricing/billing questions: route to one-pager + RevOps deal ops doc, tag @Isabelle Laurent as needed

Please reply here if you hit any rough edges today so we can capture + fix fast.

title: #docs: 1769599015
preview:
Elliot Price: need quick alignment on marketplace onboarding docs structure/IA before we lock URLs for AWS/GCP submission forms. where do we want the nav entry + where do we link security + troubleshooting?

Alex Martinez: +1. partner portals want stable URLs and we keep getting asked “link to onboarding steps” + “link to security overview”. if we change paths post-submit it’s a mess.

Wei Chen: current draft is kind of scattered (Hosted quickstart vs marketplace quickstart vs pricing pages). i’d vote for a single “Marketplace” landing page with the flow, then split by AWS/GCP.

Kira Thompson: agree. also we need to handle Dedicated/Private buyers who come in via marketplace (not just Hosted). landing page should branch by “What you bought” (Hosted usage-based vs Dedicated capacity vs Private deployment).

Ben Carter: from funnel POV, keep step count low on landing page. the most common drop is “i subscribed… now what?” so above the fold should be 3–5 steps max, then expand.

Elliot Price: proposed IA (rough):
- Getting started
  - Marketplace (landing)
    - AWS Marketplace onboarding
    - GCP Marketplace onboarding
    - Troubleshooting (entitlements/provisioning/billing)
- Security
  - Marketplace security overview (public)
  - Data handling & retention (public-ish)
  - Audit logs (public-ish)
  - Evidence (gated / on request)

Thoughts?

Naomi Feldman: security pages: we can publish a “marketplace security overview” + shared responsibility model + high-level encryption/keys. BUT evidence links should NOT be public. we can point to “SOC 2 Type II available under NDA / via trust portal” wording.

Marcus Lin: +1. also be careful with “supports” vs “enforces” language on retention (configurable vs default). pls link to the retention doc but keep it explicit what’s default and what’s configurable.

Tessa Morgan: UX note: landing page should visually mirror console screens (Subscribe → Verify entitlement → Create/link org → Create API key → First request). even if docs are text-heavy, a simple diagram helps.

Jean-Paul Laurent: if we put “Marketplace” under Getting started, can we also add a callout on the generic “Quickstart” pages that says “Bought via AWS/GCP marketplace? start here” w/ link? otherwise people will follow old quickstart and then get confused.

Alex Martinez: yes, we should do a small “marketplace entrypoint” banner on quickstarts + on pricing page.

Wei Chen: for Hosted path, AWS/GCP guides should share 80% of the same steps. can we avoid duplicate content by having one “marketplace flow” page and then AWS/GCP pages that are mostly provider-specific bits (subscription screenshot + entitlement timing + where to find IDs)?

Elliot Price: we can do:
- /marketplace (overview + common flow)
- /marketplace/aws (AWS-specific: subscribe UI, customer identifier, private offer activation, entitlement propagation notes)
- /marketplace/gcp (GCP-specific: procurement steps, billing account selection, etc)
- /marketplace/troubleshooting (shared)

Kira Thompson: pls include “what plan did I buy?” section somewhere. example: Dedicated buyers need to know they still need to talk to SE to provision capacity / region + that marketplace subscription is the procurement layer, not the infra magically appearing.

Ben Carter: +1 but keep it crisp. maybe a “Choose your deployment” section with 3 tiles: Hosted / Dedicated / Private. each tile shows: what you get immediately vs what requires coordination.

Naomi Feldman: also link security overview from the landing page, but don’t make onboarding block on it. procurement folks will go hunting though—so prominent placement is good.

Marcus Lin: troubleshooting page should include entitlement timing expectations (e.g., “can take up to X minutes”), retry guidance, and what info to include in a support ticket (org id, marketplace provider, subscription id, region). keep it practical.

Alex Martinez: we should standardize the URLs we share with partners:
1) onboarding landing
2) security overview
3) troubleshooting
That’s what AWS asked for last time.

Elliot Price: what do we call the nav item? “Marketplace onboarding” vs just “Marketplace”? (trying not to bury it)

Wei Chen: nav label “Marketplace” is fine. H1 on landing can be “Marketplace onboarding (AWS & GCP)”.

Tessa Morgan: agree. “Marketplace” in nav is easy to scan.

Kira Thompson: for troubleshooting, can we split sections by symptom?
- I subscribed but Redwood says no entitlement
- I can’t link to an existing org
- I created an org but no API key page / permissions
- Billing dimension mismatch / unexpected line items

Ben Carter: yes please. and add “still stuck? contact support” with clear routing. marketplace customers always ask “AWS or Redwood?”

Naomi Feldman: + make sure support routing doesn’t imply AWS has access to our systems. “Contact Redwood support; AWS/GCP can only help with marketplace purchase mechanics.”

Marcus Lin: also add an “Incident/status” link if we have one. entitlement sync job issues will look like user error.

Alex Martinez: URLs / redirects: we have an internal request in flight for vanity URLs. i’d like to keep these stable:
- /docs/marketplace
- /docs/marketplace/aws
- /docs/marketplace/gcp
- /docs/marketplace/troubleshooting
- /docs/security/marketplace
If we need legacy redirects from old marketplace pages, now’s the time.

Elliot Price: ok. action items:
- Elliot: update IA in docs repo + nav + stubs with final URLs (today)
- Wei/Kira: confirm content owners per page (Hosted bits vs Dedicated/Private bits) (tomorrow)
- Naomi/Marcus: review wording for security links + retention/audit log claims (this week)
- Alex: coordinate redirects/vanity URL ticket + confirm with Partnerships which exact links go into AWS/GCP submission forms

Ben Carter: pls add a short “What happens after you subscribe?” section that includes entitlement delay expectations + a link to troubleshooting. reduces support load.

Elliot Price: :thumbsup: will do. I’ll post the updated sitemap once the PR is up.

Alex Martinez: thanks. once PR is live, i’ll share with Partnerships + PM for sign-off.

Marcus Lin: ping me on the PR. I want to double check we’re not over-promising on retention defaults.

Naomi Feldman: same. also let’s keep evidence references as “available upon request” and link to the security overview page rather than a dead-end.

Wei Chen: cool. i’ll start consolidating the common flow content so AWS/GCP pages are genuinely provider-specific.

Kira Thompson: and I’ll add the Dedicated/Private callouts + “what you get immediately” section.

Elliot Price: perfect. closing loop: nav = Getting started → Marketplace; Security section includes Marketplace security overview; troubleshooting is a first-class page under Marketplace. done.

- /docs/marketplace/gcp
- /docs/marketplace/troubleshooting
- /docs/security/marketplace
If we need legacy redirects from old marketplace pages, now’s the time.

Elliot Price: ok. action items:
- Elliot: update IA in docs repo + nav + stubs with final URLs (today)
- Wei/Kira: confirm content owners per page (Hosted bits vs Dedicated/Private bits) (tomorrow)
- Naomi/Marcus: review wording for security links + retention/audit log claims (this week)
- Alex: coordinate redirects/vanity URL ticket + confirm with Partnerships which exact links go into AWS/GCP submission forms

Ben Carter: pls add a short “What happens after you subscribe?” section that includes entitlement delay expectations + a link to troubleshooting. reduces support load.

Elliot Price: :thumbsup: will do. I’ll post the updated sitemap once the PR is up.

Alex Martinez: thanks. once PR is live, i’ll share with Partnerships + PM for sign-off.

Marcus Lin: ping me on the PR. I want to double check we’re not over-promising on retention defaults.

Naomi Feldman: same. also let’s keep evidence references as “available upon request” and link to the security overview page rather than a dead-end.

Wei Chen: cool. i’ll start consolidating the common flow content so AWS/GCP pages are genuinely provider-specific.

Kira Thompson: and I’ll add the Dedicated/Private callouts + “what you get immediately” section.

Elliot Price: perfect. closing loop: nav = Getting started → Marketplace; Security section includes Marketplace security overview; troubleshooting is a first-class page under Marketplace. done.

title: #customer-success: 1769461199
preview:
Jasmine Liu: Kicking off a draft “Marketplace Customer Onboarding Playbook” for CS/Support. Goal: make it super clear what’s self-serve vs when we open a Support ticket vs when we pull in SE. Also need a short escalation matrix we can paste into internal handbook.

Rachel Kim: +1. We keep getting the same questions: entitlement delays, “I subscribed but can’t create org / no quota”, private offer activation timing, and billing dims mismatch. We should standardize the triage path.

Dev Patel: Yep. From Support side I want 2 things:
1) macros we can use (copy/paste)
2) explicit criteria when to route to SE vs keep in Support vs open Eng escalation.

Markus Klein: SE perspective: we’re happy to help when it’s blocking a big eval/POC, but a lot of these are pure entitlement plumbing. If we define “SE triggers” based on deal size / Dedicated/Private / tight timeline, it’ll save churn.

Alex Martinez: For docs, we can link the playbook to the marketplace onboarding guides once URLs are final. If we can keep the playbook structured around the customer journey (subscribe → entitlement → org provisioning → API key → first call), it’ll map cleanly to docs pages.

Jasmine Liu: Draft outline (internal):
A) Intake: confirm marketplace (AWS vs GCP), subscription ID, customer email domain, Redwood org (if exists)
B) Day 0 onboarding steps + common blockers
C) “When to open a Support ticket” (SUP) vs “SE engagement” vs “Security/GRC”
D) Escalation matrix + SLAs
E) Comms templates (customer-facing)

Ben Carter: For funnel instrumentation we need to be consistent on the “blocker categories” too. Even if it’s a Support ticket, tag it so we can track dropoffs.

Chloe Martin: FYI console onboarding errors we’re showing:
- `ENTITLEMENT_NOT_FOUND`
- `ENTITLEMENT_PENDING_PROPAGATION`
- `ORG_LINK_CONFLICT`
- `SUBSCRIPTION_ALREADY_LINKED`
If the playbook uses the same wording, it’ll reduce confusion.

Dev Patel: Can we agree on default waiting guidance? Right now reps are saying everything from “5 minutes” to “24 hours”.

Markus Klein: AWS is usually pretty fast, but propagation delays do happen. GCP has been slower in my experience. We should be careful not to promise a hard number.

Rachel Kim: Proposal:
- First-touch guidance: “Entitlement can take up to ~30–60 minutes to propagate. If you’re still blocked after 60 minutes, contact Support and include your Marketplace subscription ID.”
- Internally: if >60 min, open SUP ticket + check entitlement sync logs.

Chloe Martin: That aligns with what we’ve seen in dry runs. We also need to tell customers to hit “Retry entitlement check” (button) and not create a second org.

Jasmine Liu: Ok writing this down as policy:
1) If customer subscribed <60 min ago → Support responds with “pending propagation” macro + asks for subscription ID + suggests retry.
2) If >60 min and still `ENTITLEMENT_NOT_FOUND` → Support opens SUP ticket (queue: Marketplace Onboarding), assigns severity based on impact.
3) If customer is enterprise + time-bound launch → SE can parallel-path to keep momentum, but ticket still gets opened.

Dev Patel: Please define severity:
- Sev3: single customer blocked onboarding, no workaround
- Sev2: multiple customers or known incident (entitlement sync degraded)
- Sev1: widespread outage / sync job failing globally
Also: whenever Sev2+, page Eng on-call.

Rachel Kim: Add “workarounds” section:
- Confirm they used correct cloud account (AWS payer account vs linked account issues)
- Confirm region (they might be trying to provision in a region we don’t support for that SKU)
- If they already have a Redwood org, they must LINK not CREATE

Markus Klein: SE triggers (suggested):
- Dedicated or Private SKU interest (they bought Hosted but want Dedicated/Private evaluation)
- Private offer / committed spend negotiation in-flight
- Customer needs architecture review (VPC, KMS, audit logs) *and* has a security meeting scheduled
- Customer is >$XXK/yr forecast OR exec-sponsored
(We can fill the $ threshold after Jordan/Steph align.)

Jasmine Liu: Love it. Let’s also explicitly say: “SE is not required for basic Hosted API onboarding.” Otherwise we keep getting pulled in for password resets :)

Alex Martinez: Docs linkage ideas:
- “How marketplace onboarding works” page
- “Troubleshooting entitlements” page
- “Security for marketplace buyers” page
In playbook we can use short links + internal notes like “If docs are outdated, file a Docs issue + paste screenshot of console error.”

Ben Carter: Tagging: can we standardize ticket tags? e.g.
- `mktpl-entitlement-delay`
- `mktpl-org-linking`
- `mktpl-api-key`
- `mktpl-billing-dimension`
This helps metrics.

Dev Patel: Also billing dimension mismatch should *not* go to SE first. That’s Support → Billing/Eng. We’ve had cases where customer sees unexpected dimension name in AWS bill.

Rachel Kim: Yes. Add rule:
- Billing/reporting confusion → Support first.
- If it’s a contract interpretation / PO language → route to Partnerships/Finance (not SE).

Jasmine Liu: Ok, customer-facing macros (draft language):
1) Pending propagation: “Thanks — marketplace entitlements can take some time to propagate. Please click Retry in the Redwood Console. If you’re still blocked after ~60 minutes from subscription, reply with your subscription ID and we’ll investigate.”
2) Org linking conflict: “It looks like this subscription is already linked to a Redwood org. If you intended to use a different org, tell us the org slug and we can help verify the correct linkage.”
3) Private offer activation: “Private offers may take additional time to activate on the marketplace side. If you share the offer ID / subscription ID, we can confirm receipt and expected activation window.”

Chloe Martin: Add a line telling them where to find subscription ID (AWS console / GCP marketplace page). People keep sending screenshots of the wrong page.

Markus Klein: For enterprise onboarding, SE should provide a “first-call” validation + best practices (rate limits, streaming, retries). But again, only after entitlement is confirmed.

Dev Patel: Operational detail: Support should always ask for:
- Marketplace: AWS/GCP
- Subscription ID
- Redwood org slug (if any)
- Console error code
- Time of subscription (approx)
Then we can reproduce faster.

Jasmine Liu: Action items:
- Jasmine: turn this into a 1-pager playbook + escalation matrix by EOW
- Dev: draft macros in Support tool + propose tags
- Markus: propose SE trigger thresholds + owner list (rotation)
- Alex: confirm docs URLs + where playbook should live
- Chloe/Ben: confirm error code strings + instrumentation alignment

Rachel Kim: :thumbsup: Once there’s a draft, I’ll sanity check it against recent onboarding cases + add “do/don’t” notes for comms (avoid promising exact approval timelines).

title: #announcements: 1768700100
preview:
Ben Carter: :rocket: Rolling out Lifecycle Growth Experiments (week 2 launch)

Starting today we’re shipping:
1) Onboarding email drip v2 (new users) — A/B test (Variant A vs B)
2) Initial in-console onboarding nudge experiment (behind flags)

Why: reduce time-to-first-token + lift activation (% of new signups hitting first successful request + first 100k tokens in 7 days).

What’s live (high-level):
• Email v2: refreshed copy + CTAs aligned to the minimal “hello world” request + quickstart links
• Console: “Next step” nudges for: create API key → make first request → enable streaming → view usage dashboard

Guardrails / how we’re doing this safely:
• Deliverability ramp: starting at low volume and scaling daily if complaint/bounce stays within thresholds
• Suppression: no sends to unsubscribed users, existing active users, and anyone flagged as sales-assisted/active opp (per exclusions)
• Holdout: a % of eligible new signups will be held out for clean measurement
• Stop/go: we pause if we see deliverability issues or meaningful negative movement in activation funnel

Opt-out / compliance reminders:
• All lifecycle emails include unsubscribe link (CAN-SPAM) + we respect preferences immediately
• We now have a Console email preferences surface (Settings → Email preferences) so users can manage onboarding/lifecycle sends
• GDPR/data handling: audience membership is based only on approved product events + retention limits per policy

Where to monitor:
• Activation Funnel Dashboard: https://observability.redwood.local/d/activation-funnel (signup → api key → first request → 100k tokens)
• Email metrics (opens/clicks/complaints): https://console.redwood.local/marketing/email-health

Escalation paths:
• Deliverability/complaints: @Tyler Benson + @Ben Carter (and #marketing)
• Console nudge bugs/UX regressions: @Marissa Cole + @Dylan Brooks (and #product)
• Data/metrics discrepancies: @Nadia Rahman + @Sergio Costa (and #eng-platform)

If you notice anything weird (duplicate emails, missing emails, nudges showing up in the wrong place), reply in thread with screenshots + user/org id if you have it. Thanks all.

(reactions: :thumbsup: x14, :eyes: x6)

Camila Reyes: Thanks Ben. +1 on watching the guardrails closely this week. If we hit deliverability warning signs, we pause first and debug second.

Marissa Cole: Console nudge is live for the targeted cohort only (new signups, eligible routes). Placement is: Console home + API Keys page. Dismissal should persist per user.

Dylan Brooks: Flags for anyone curious:
• `lifecycle_growth_email_v2_ab` (cohort assign + holdout)
• `console_onboarding_nudges_v1` (nudge eligibility)
We’re at 10% ramp on console exposure right now. Will move to 25% tomorrow if error rate stays flat.

Tyler Benson: Deliverability note: we’re throttling at the MTA + monitoring complaint rate hourly. If you see anyone internal not receiving, check spam first but also drop the address in thread and I’ll verify suppression + DMARC alignment.

Olga Petrov: Reminder from compliance side: please don’t manually add anyone to an audience outside the approved rules (especially EU users). If a customer asks about why they’re seeing onboarding emails, point them to Email preferences + we can share the data handling summary.

Michael Grant: +1. Also ensure the “from” name + physical address footer stays intact in the templates. Don’t edit copy directly in the sending tool without a review loop.

Allison Grant: Dashboard panels include alerts for sudden activation drops post-launch. If we see a step-change in first request completion, we’ll page #eng-platform for validation.

Sergio Costa: FYI: there’s a known 10–15 min lag on the funnel dashboard right now. Not a regression, just pipeline delay. If you see missing events beyond that window, tag me.

Leila Farouk: QA checklist for console nudges is running now (cross-browser + mobile). If anyone hits a nudge blocking the API key flow, please treat as P0 and ping me + Dylan.

Nadia Rahman: Instrumentation sanity checks: signup + api_key_created are deduped; first_request is per org+model+day. If you’re comparing raw event counts vs funnel uniques, that’s why.

Support Bot: Heads up: Support will monitor for any spike in “didn’t receive onboarding email” tickets today/tomorrow and route to @Tyler Benson + @Ben Carter. If you see customer complaints in Slack, please link the ticket.

Ben Carter: Appreciate all. We’ll post a short status update in #announcements end of day with: ramp %, any incidents, and early directional metrics (no overfitting to tiny samples). Keep the reports coming.

title: #general: 1768617777
preview:
Ben Carter: looking for a few volunteers to dogfood the new self-serve onboarding flow this week 🙏

Goal: catch friction before we ramp lifecycle emails + console nudges to a bigger cohort.

What to do (10-15 min):
1) create a *fresh* Hosted API account (use a personal email alias like `name+dogfood@...` or a throwaway)
2) go through console onboarding steps (API key creation → first request)
3) try the “hello world” snippet + streaming toggle
4) sanity check you received the onboarding email(s) + that unsubscribe/preferences link works

Drop feedback here as you go. If you hit anything blocker-y, pls include:
- what page/step
- screenshot if easy
- browser + region

Links:
- console: https://console.redwoodinference.com/
- quickstart snippet: https://docs.redwoodinference.com/quickstart

(we’re testing feature-flagged nudges; if you don’t see them, say so)

(reactions: :eyes: x14 :thumbsup: x9)

Marissa Cole: +1 — especially want feedback on the checklist/nudges behavior (when they appear, when they disappear, whether copy/CTAs are clear). If something feels spammy/confusing, call it out.

Dylan Brooks: if you see a nudge in a weird place or it “sticks” after you complete the step, pls mention the URL + what you clicked. We’re persisting dismissals and completion states and want to validate it’s not buggy.

Tyler Benson: for email deliverability checks: if you *don’t* get the first onboarding email within ~5 min of signup, note your email provider (gmail/outlook/etc). We’re watching bounces/complaints so we’re ramping slowly.

Kenji Nakamura: if anyone has time, also check mobile responsiveness on the console steps. Doesn’t need to be perfect but want to catch any CTA overflow / weird spacing.

Leila Farouk: volunteering 🙋‍♀️ will run through on Chrome + Safari and try at least 1 mobile pass. Will report back with screenshots.

Nadia Rahman: I can do an instrumentation sanity pass too — will check that events fire for `api_key_created` and `first_request_succeeded` (at least from the UI). If I see gaps I’ll flag.

Chloe Martin: I’ll take one pass from EU VPN to see if anything region-specific breaks (we had a couple eventing quirks last month). Will post if I see missing nudges or slow loads.

Paula Mendes: if you’re already a Redwood user and don’t want a brand new acct, DM me and I can add you to the dogfood cohort flag. But prefer fresh signup if possible so we test the real path.

RedwoodBot: Ben Carter added a reminder: "Dogfood onboarding flow — share friction points" (in 1 day)

Leila Farouk: ran through (Chrome, macOS). Notes:
- Signup ok.
- Landed in console, saw checklist with 3 steps. API key CTA clear.
- After creating key, checklist updated *but* the "Make your first request" nudge didn’t appear until I refreshed the page.
- Copy suggestion: nudge CTA says “Run example” but it opens docs in new tab — I expected it to run in-console.
- Got first email ~2 min later (gmail). Unsubscribe link present.

Dylan Brooks: thx Leila — the refresh requirement is not intended. Can you share the URL you were on when you created the API key? Also did you navigate via sidebar or stay on the same page?

Leila Farouk: stayed on /api-keys, clicked “Create key”, then it showed success toast. Checklist updated in right panel, but the next nudge didn’t show until I went back to /overview and refreshed.

Marissa Cole: that sounds like we’re not emitting the completion signal to the checklist component consistently. Dylan I can open a bug w/ repro steps if helpful.

Nadia Rahman: quick check on events: I saw `api_key_created` in the client logs, but didn’t see `onboarding_checklist_step_completed` until I navigated away. Might be related.

Paula Mendes: +1 — sounds like an event timing issue (page-level flush). Dylan/Nadia, pls file in ENG queue so we don’t lose it.

Chloe Martin: EU VPN pass (FF + Chrome):
- Nudges did show for me without needing refresh.
- BUT the "first request" docs page loaded slowly and code block formatting was busted on Firefox (indentation weird). Might be docs CSS.
- Email arrived ~6 min later (outlook). Went to "Focused" not junk.

Tyler Benson: outlook arrival time is a bit high but still ok for ramp phase. If anyone sees >10 min, please flag. Also: pls don’t click unsubscribe unless you’re using a throwaway — it will suppress future lifecycle sends.

Kenji Nakamura: for the docs CSS, can you paste the exact page URL? I’ll ping DevEx/docs.

Chloe Martin: https://docs.redwoodinference.com/quickstart#first-request — code block has a horizontal scroll on FF and the copy button overlaps.

Ben Carter: super helpful, thanks all. Keep the feedback coming — especially any “wait, what do I do next?” moments or anything that feels misleading. If we can shave even 1-2 steps to first successful request, it’s a win.

Ben Carter: also, if anyone is up for it: try the path where you *don’t* create an API key immediately and see if the console nudges + email are appropriately guiding you vs nagging.

Leila Farouk: tried the “stall” path: left after signup, came back ~30 min later. Console remembered checklist state. I got 1 reminder email ~1 hr later. It was helpful but subject line felt a bit salesy (“Unlock faster inference…”). Might want more “here’s the exact next step” vibe.

Marissa Cole: noted on subject line tone — Soojin’s team has some variants, we can steer to more action-oriented.

Tyler Benson: +1, we can segment subject line by stalled vs activated if needed. Also we should avoid anything that implies performance guarantees.

Dylan Brooks: filed bug for refresh issue + event flush timing. Will update when patch is out.

Ben Carter: thanks everyone. If you ran the flow, pls add a :white_check_mark: reaction to this message so we can track coverage. (reactions: :white_check_mark: x8)

Goal: catch friction before we ramp lifecycle emails + console nudges to a bigger cohort.

What to do (10-15 min):
1) create a *fresh* Hosted API account (use a personal email alias like `name+dogfood@...` or a throwaway)
2) go through console onboarding steps (API key creation → first request)
3) try the “hello world” snippet + streaming toggle
4) sanity check you received the onboarding email(s) + that unsubscribe/preferences link works

Drop feedback here as you go. If you hit anything blocker-y, pls include:
- what page/step
- screenshot if easy
- browser + region

Links:
- console: https://console.redwoodinference.com/
- quickstart snippet: https://docs.redwoodinference.com/quickstart

(we’re testing feature-flagged nudges; if you don’t see them, say so)

(reactions: :eyes: x14 :thumbsup: x9)

Marissa Cole: +1 — especially want feedback on the checklist/nudges behavior (when they appear, when they disappear, whether copy/CTAs are clear). If something feels spammy/confusing, call it out.

Dylan Brooks: if you see a nudge in a weird place or it “sticks” after you complete the step, pls mention the URL + what you clicked. We’re persisting dismissals and completion states and want to validate it’s not buggy.

Tyler Benson: for email deliverability checks: if you *don’t* get the first onboarding email within ~5 min of signup, note your email provider (gmail/outlook/etc). We’re watching bounces/complaints so we’re ramping slowly.

Kenji Nakamura: if anyone has time, also check mobile responsiveness on the console steps. Doesn’t need to be perfect but want to catch any CTA overflow / weird spacing.

Leila Farouk: volunteering 🙋‍♀️ will run through on Chrome + Safari and try at least 1 mobile pass. Will report back with screenshots.

Nadia Rahman: I can do an instrumentation sanity pass too — will check that events fire for `api_key_created` and `first_request_succeeded` (at least from the UI). If I see gaps I’ll flag.

Chloe Martin: I’ll take one pass from EU VPN to see if anything region-specific breaks (we had a couple eventing quirks last month). Will post if I see missing nudges or slow loads.

Paula Mendes: if you’re already a Redwood user and don’t want a brand new acct, DM me and I can add you to the dogfood cohort flag. But prefer fresh signup if possible so we test the real path.

RedwoodBot: Ben Carter added a reminder: "Dogfood onboarding flow — share friction points" (in 1 day)

Leila Farouk: ran through (Chrome, macOS). Notes:
- Signup ok.
- Landed in console, saw checklist with 3 steps. API key CTA clear.
- After creating key, checklist updated *but* the "Make your first request" nudge didn’t appear until I refreshed the page.
- Copy suggestion: nudge CTA says “Run example” but it opens docs in new tab — I expected it to run in-console.
- Got first email ~2 min later (gmail). Unsubscribe link present.

Dylan Brooks: thx Leila — the refresh requirement is not intended. Can you share the URL you were on when you created the API key? Also did you navigate via sidebar or stay on the same page?

Leila Farouk: stayed on /api-keys, clicked “Create key”, then it showed success toast. Checklist updated in right panel, but the next nudge didn’t show until I went back to /overview and refreshed.

Marissa Cole: that sounds like we’re not emitting the completion signal to the checklist component consistently. Dylan I can open a bug w/ repro steps if helpful.

Nadia Rahman: quick check on events: I saw `api_key_created` in the client logs, but didn’t see `onboarding_checklist_step_completed` until I navigated away. Might be related.

Paula Mendes: +1 — sounds like an event timing issue (page-level flush). Dylan/Nadia, pls file in ENG queue so we don’t lose it.

Chloe Martin: EU VPN pass (FF + Chrome):
- Nudges did show for me without needing refresh.
- BUT the "first request" docs page loaded slowly and code block formatting was busted on Firefox (indentation weird). Might be docs CSS.
- Email arrived ~6 min later (outlook). Went to "Focused" not junk.

Tyler Benson: outlook arrival time is a bit high but still ok for ramp phase. If anyone sees >10 min, please flag. Also: pls don’t click unsubscribe unless you’re using a throwaway — it will suppress future lifecycle sends.

Kenji Nakamura: for the docs CSS, can you paste the exact page URL? I’ll ping DevEx/docs.

Chloe Martin: https://docs.redwoodinference.com/quickstart#first-request — code block has a horizontal scroll on FF and the copy button overlaps.

Ben Carter: super helpful, thanks all. Keep the feedback coming — especially any “wait, what do I do next?” moments or anything that feels misleading. If we can shave even 1-2 steps to first successful request, it’s a win.

Ben Carter: also, if anyone is up for it: try the path where you *don’t* create an API key immediately and see if the console nudges + email are appropriately guiding you vs nagging.

Leila Farouk: tried the “stall” path: left after signup, came back ~30 min later. Console remembered checklist state. I got 1 reminder email ~1 hr later. It was helpful but subject line felt a bit salesy (“Unlock faster inference…”). Might want more “here’s the exact next step” vibe.

Marissa Cole: noted on subject line tone — Soojin’s team has some variants, we can steer to more action-oriented.

Tyler Benson: +1, we can segment subject line by stalled vs activated if needed. Also we should avoid anything that implies performance guarantees.

Dylan Brooks: filed bug for refresh issue + event flush timing. Will update when patch is out.

Ben Carter: thanks everyone. If you ran the flow, pls add a :white_check_mark: reaction to this message so we can track coverage. (reactions: :white_check_mark: x8)

title: #help: 1768621199
preview:
Ben Carter: hey folks — seeing a weird tracking discrepancy in the activation funnel dashboard. signup → api_key_created looks fine globally, but first_request + first_100k_tokens drop to ~0 for EU traffic since ~this morning.

Ben Carter: this is for the lifecycle growth sprint dashboards. I’m worried we’re missing events in one region vs an actual activation regression. anyone know how to interpret / who can help debug?

Tyler Benson: +1, I noticed the same when slicing by `request_region=eu-west`. NA looks normal. UTM traffic is still there so not a top-of-funnel issue.

Mei Lin: can you paste the panel / filter you’re looking at? is it filtered on `deployment_mode=hosted` + `source=console` or all sources?

Ben Carter: panel is “Activation Funnel (Signup → First Request)” in the new pack. breakdown by region. filter is `deployment_mode=hosted` only.

Sergio Costa: the dashboard panels use `activation.first_request` as the canonical event name (post-standardization). if EU is near-zero it’s usually one of:
- property mismatch causing the region breakdown to bucket as null
- ingestion delay for the EU collector topic
- event name drift (older event name still emitted)

Sergio Costa: do you see EU activity if you remove the region breakdown and just look at total events?

Tyler Benson: total looks slightly down but not near-zero. it’s specifically “EU region” line that’s flat.

Nadia Rahman: that smells like property mismatch. we recently made `request_region` required, but the fallback logic differs between collectors.

Nadia Rahman: quick sanity checks:
1) in raw events (Snowflake) for the last 2h, do you see `activation.first_request` with `request_region` null?
2) did we change the allowed values? (e.g. `eu-west-1` vs `eu-west`)

Logan Wright: looping in. we had a known edge where the EU edge gateway emits `region` not `request_region` on a subset of API paths (streaming endpoint) — thought it was fixed in last week’s patch though.

Logan Wright: Ben, are these missing events mostly coming from streaming? (people following the new “enable streaming” CTA?)

Ben Carter: good call. this is mostly new signups following the email “try streaming” step + console snippet. so yeah likely streaming path.

Allison Grant: also check if the dashboard is filtering on `sdk_language` or `client=typescript` etc. sometimes the SDK starts sending a different shape and the panel filter excludes it.

Mei Lin: I can run a quick query on the raw table. what’s the time window you first noticed?

Tyler Benson: started ~09:30 PT. was normal yesterday.

RWD-Deploy Bot: Deployment notice: `events-collector` rolled out to `eu-west` at 2026-01-17 09:18 PT. Version `collector@3.19.4`.

Nadia Rahman: ^ that deploy timing is suspicious.

Logan Wright: yep. likely collector regression for property mapping. I’ll check the rollout notes / diffs.

Mei Lin: ran quick sample (last 60m): `activation.first_request` events exist for EU traffic but `request_region` is null on ~78% of them. those same rows have `region=eu-west-1`.

Mei Lin: so the events are there; the region breakdown in the dashboard is dropping them into null (and funnel step filtering might be excluding null depending on panel).

Sergio Costa: confirms it. I’ll update the dashboard to coalesce `request_region` with `region` temporarily so Growth isn’t blind while we fix at source.

Allison Grant: +1 but please also add an alert on “% null request_region by collector region” — this will bite us again.

Logan Wright: action items:
- I’ll open an INT ticket + assign to collector owners. we need EU collector to normalize into `request_region`.
- Mei, can you share the query + a couple event ids in the ticket?

Mei Lin: yep, will do. also looks like it’s specifically from `/v1/chat/completions:stream` path. non-streaming has correct property.

Ben Carter: amazing, thanks all. for reporting: should we treat EU activation as “unknown” for today vs true regression?

Nadia Rahman: treat as tracking issue (events present, property missing). activation volume is likely fine but the region slice is broken. total funnel should still be directionally ok if it doesn’t require region.

Tyler Benson: makes sense. I’ll annotate today’s readout + hold off on any decision based on EU-only segment.

Sergio Costa: I’ll post back once the dashboard patch is live (ETA ~30m).

Logan Wright: INT ticket incoming. Ben/Tyler — if you need a clean number, use total funnel w/o region breakdown until fix lands.

Ben Carter: perfect. appreciate the fast triage.
