Markus Klein: From SE side — if the signal is “Private/VPC intent” or “security review content,” please loop an SE early. The playbook has a checklist + who to pull in.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Privacy reminder: please don’t copy/paste customer prompts or outputs into HubSpot notes/Slack when discussing a signal. The pipeline is designed to be aggregate-only; keep follow-up notes high-level.

Camila Reyes: Are we tracking outcomes somewhere consistent? (meeting booked / disqualified / opp created)

Isabelle Laurent: @Camila Reyes yes — in HubSpot we added a required dropdown on the PLG task close: Outcome = {Meeting booked, No response, Disqualified, Existing customer/CS-led, Routed to SE, Other}. That’s what we’ll use for attribution.

Logan Wright: Eng tidbit: aggregation is daily, so signals won’t be “real-time” minute-by-minute. If you see a task, it’s based on sustained activity over a window (helps reduce noise).

Talia Benson: If anyone wants to see the rollout plan + weekly review cadence, it’s in PM-496. We’ll do a weekly 30 min “signal quality” review with Sales/Growth/Ops.

Ben Carter: Thanks all. Keep examples coming — especially the “this would have been helpful” *and* the “this was spammy” ones. That’s how we tune thresholds + suppression.

title: #customer-success: 1747188408
preview:
Rachel Kim: Kicking off QBR planning for our first regulated pilot (likely Northbridge). Goal: tell a tight story that supports expansion (Dedicated/Private) + gets Security comfortable. Can we align on (1) metrics to report, (2) who needs to be in the room, (3) narrative arc / slide outline? :memo:

Jasmine Liu: +1. Treat this as “pilot closeout + exec-ready expansion proposal” vs a generic QBR. I’d like to walk out with an agreed next phase (production rollout + capacity plan) and a timeline that Procurement can map to.

Liam O'Rourke: For stakeholders: customer side we should ask for (a) platform owner, (b) security lead who reviewed us, (c) app owner for the initial use case, and ideally (d) someone w/ budget authority. Internal: AE (Stephanie), SE (Evan/Markus), Security (Olga or Aisha depending on level), and me/Rachel for CS.

Rachel Kim: Cool. On metrics—what are must-haves that resonate for regulated buyers?

Evan Scott: Performance: 
- p50/p95 end-to-end latency (include streaming TTFB if we have it)
- throughput at steady-state (req/min or tokens/sec) for the pilot traffic pattern
- reliability: error rate + any incidents / mitigations
- cost: effective $/1M tokens (or whatever unit they care about), and what batching/caching did

Evan Scott: Also: “time to integrate” (days to first response in their env), and ops time saved (we handled X% of routing/rollout vs DIY). Those land well.

Markus Klein: For narrative, I’d put “economics + control” upfront:
1) Why they evaluated (latency targets + predictable throughput + compliance controls)
2) What we deployed (topology: VPC/private vs dedicated), what they owned vs what we owned
3) Results (benchmarks + SLOs)
4) Compliance posture recap (auditability, encryption/KMS, identity)
5) Recommendation: expand scope + production plan

Olga Petrov: Compliance section needs to be specific but careful. We should report:
- audit logs: what events/fields they reviewed + how to export
- retention: what’s configurable vs fixed defaults (avoid over-claiming)
- identity: SSO/SAML status (pilot learnings) and any SCIM expectations
- encryption: at-rest/in-transit + KMS option (customer-managed keys if applicable)
And we should include “open items + owners + dates” so it doesn’t feel hand-wavy.

Rachel Kim: +1 on “open items.” If we list them ourselves it builds trust.

Stephanie Nguyen: From AE POV: we should end with a very explicit “expansion ask.” Example: “approve production pilot phase 2 for 2 apps + reserve capacity for X TPS” and “security sign-off on evidence pack items A/B/C.” Otherwise the meeting drifts.

Ben Carter: Marketing/ABM angle: if they’re willing, we should ask for a *generic* reference statement (no logo) about: “met latency + audit requirements in VPC deployment.” Even a 1–2 sentence quote helps future regulated pipeline.

Daniel Carter: Evidence pack: we can include a final slide “What we provided” (SOC2 process, architecture diagrams, sample audit logs) + what’s available under NDA. I can prep a checklist so we don’t accidentally promise artifacts we can’t share broadly.

Rachel Kim: Ok, so metrics list shaping up:
- SLOs: p50/p95 latency, error rate, uptime during pilot window
- Throughput: tokens/sec at target context lengths
- Cost: effective unit cost before/after batching/caching + drivers
- Integration: time to deploy in their env + # of engineering hours on each side (estimate)
- Compliance: audit log coverage + export, identity, encryption/KMS, retention/residency notes
- Operational: rollback/fallback story + how we’d run it in prod (monitoring, alerts)
Anything missing?

Evan Scott: Add “quality” even if lightweight: show they ran a fixed eval set and we matched/beat baseline acceptance rate. Doesn’t need deep ML metrics—just “no regression vs current provider/model”.

Markus Klein: + a slide on “what would have broken us” (risk register + mitigations). Regulated execs like seeing that we thought about failure modes (capacity constraints, region isolation, incident response, audit gaps).

Olga Petrov: If we do risk slide, include incident response contact + response time expectations (not SLA language, just process). Also call out access controls: RBAC in Console + audit trail for admin actions.

Jasmine Liu: Agenda suggestion (45 min):
- 5 min: objectives + recap
- 10 min: architecture + what we tested
- 15 min: results (performance/economics/quality)
- 10 min: security/compliance review summary + remaining items
- 5 min: next phase plan + decision asks

Rachel Kim: Who should present what?

Stephanie Nguyen: I can open/close + run the “decision asks / timeline” slide.

Evan Scott: I’ll cover pilot setup + performance results. I can also demo dashboards if needed (but better: screenshots).

Olga Petrov: I’ll take the compliance summary slide(s), but I want to review wording before it goes out. Please route the draft through me.

Markus Klein: I can do “production architecture” and “what changes in prod” (SLO monitoring, fallback policies, scaling plan).

Daniel Carter: I’ll provide evidence pack checklist slide + what’s shareable under NDA.

Rachel Kim: Great. Invite list (internal): Rachel (host), Jasmine, Stephanie, Evan, Markus, Olga, Daniel. Optional: Dr. Aisha Rahman if they bring their CISO / if security feels tense.

Olga Petrov: Agree. If their security lead attends, I’m enough. If they escalate to CISO, Aisha should join.

Ben Carter: Can we add Tyler (ops/attribution) only if we’re asking for the reference quote? Otherwise keep it tight.

Jasmine Liu: Keep it tight.

Rachel Kim: Action items:
- Rachel: send customer invite suggestion + proposed agenda to Stephanie by EOD tomorrow
- Evan: draft metrics slide (latency/throughput/cost/quality) + screenshots by Friday
- Markus: prod architecture + rollout plan slide by Friday
- Olga: compliance summary + open items/owners table by Monday
- Daniel: evidence pack slide + NDA sharing note by Monday

Stephanie Nguyen: Works. Also please add one slide that clearly states “Dedicated vs Private recommendation” + why (data boundary, residency, control plane). We keep getting stuck there.

Markus Klein: I’ll add that. We can frame as “current: VPC private deployment pattern validated” and “next: lock capacity + support tier” (tie to Dedicated/Private packaging without getting into pricing).

Olga Petrov: Reminder: avoid terms like “HIPAA certified” / “HIPAA compliant” in the deck. Say “supports PHI-adjacent workloads with customer-configured controls” (or similar) depending on vertical. For Northbridge, use “regulated financial data” language.

Rachel Kim: Noted. I’ll add a “do not say” note in speaker notes.

Evan Scott: One more: include a slide showing what we changed based on their feedback (e.g., SAML metadata validation fix/workaround). Demonstrates responsiveness and reduces perceived risk.

Rachel Kim: Great call. That’ll also help Procurement narrative.

Stephanie Nguyen: :thumbsup: If we can get the QBR on calendar for next week, that keeps momentum while security review is still fresh.

Rachel Kim: I’ll coordinate scheduling once we have a first draft deck. Will post here with a link when ready.

title: #marketing: 1746814820
preview:
Camila Reyes: Week 1 pilot readout (NA cohort) — signal volume is way higher than we expected and it’s starting to feel spammy. Dropping quick stats + proposed fixes.

Camila Reyes: Metrics (Mon–Sun):
- 46 companies got at least 1 routed PLG signal into HubSpot
- 112 tasks created (avg 2.4/company)
- 27 companies had >1 signal family in the same 48h (e.g., “usage spike” + “docs intent” + “429s”)
- 9/112 tasks were marked “not relevant” by AEs in notes (unstructured) + we had 1 explicit “why are you emailing me” complaint come through Support

Camila Reyes: What’s driving noise:
1) Intent signals are too “hair trigger” (single docs view / pricing click)
2) Re-notification is too short (we’re creating new tasks within 48–72h for the same account)
3) Marketing nurture touches are colliding w/ AE outreach (we’re effectively double-pinging)

Tyler Benson: +1. Pulled a quick slice from the workflow logs: 18 companies had a routed task within 24h of receiving a lifecycle email from our “Scale your inference” nurture. That’s basically guaranteed to look uncoordinated.

Ben Carter: Agree the top culprit is intent. Right now it’s effectively: ANY of {Dedicated docs, Private docs, Security FAQ, Pricing} => intent=true.
We should require either (a) 2+ intent events in 7d OR (b) 1 intent event + a real threshold signal.

Isabelle Laurent: From Sales Ops POV: the multiple tasks per company is the bigger pain than “false positives”. AEs are seeing 3 tasks w/ slightly different names and no clear priority. We need (1) grouping, (2) suppression window, (3) one “owner-facing” next step.

Stephanie Nguyen: This matches AE feedback. Avery said some of these are “student project” accounts clicking around docs. They don’t belong in the same funnel as real expansion candidates.

Avery Johnson: yep. I had 4 tasks last week that were literally: pricing page + security faq. No usage, no errors, no team expansion. I didn’t know what to do other than ignore.

Nikhil Sharma: On the product side: we can tighten the signal definitions in scoring service fairly quickly. The PRD called out “avoid vanity signals” and we’re seeing exactly that.

Camila Reyes: Proposed changes (please react / comment):
A) Raise thresholds (scale + reliability)
- Usage spike: change from “>2x 7d avg” to “>3x 14d avg AND absolute >= 5M tokens/day for 3 consecutive days”
- Spend velocity: require >=$1.5k in trailing 7d OR >=$5k trailing 30d (currently too low)
- 429s: require >= 50 429s/day for 2 days OR “429 rate >1% of requests” (not raw count only)

B) Tighten intent
- Dedicated/Private intent requires 2+ high-intent events in 7d (examples: “Dedicated pricing guidance” + “Dedicated docs”, “Security FAQ” + “VPC/Private docs”)
- Pricing page alone should NOT qualify
- Security FAQ alone should NOT qualify unless paired with VPC/Private docs OR enterprise login/SAML docs

C) Suppression windows / dedupe
- 14-day suppression per company per signal family (scale/reliability/compliance)
- If a task exists and is open, do not create another; update the existing task note instead
- Max 1 new task per company per 7d across ALL signal families (unless compliance signal is present)

D) Marketing touch suppression
- If marketing lifecycle email sent in last 72h, don’t create AE task unless threshold signal is “high” (>=$5k 30d or >=10M tokens/day)
- Add a property we can check: last_marketing_touch_ts (Tyler, can we stamp this via workflow?)

Tyler Benson: For D: yes, we can stamp something like `last_mktg_touch_date` on company record from HubSpot side. But if the router is creating tasks externally, we need it to read that property before task creation.

David Kim: That’s doable. The router already does company lookup for ownership. We can add a guard: if last_mktg_touch_date within X hours and signal_score < threshold => suppress. We’ll need agreement on which property + format.

Ben Carter: Re A: I’d be careful on hard $ thresholds because we’ll miss high-volume free-credit accounts that are clearly scaling. Maybe “$ OR tokens” as you wrote.
Also we should treat embeddings separately; some of the “tokens/day” logic doesn’t map cleanly.

Camila Reyes: Good call. Let’s split “generation tokens” vs “embedding units” in the scoring output and set distinct cut lines.

Isabelle Laurent: Suggest a simple prioritization rule for the task title/body:
- If compliance intent present => “Compliance / Private interest” (highest)
- Else if reliability (429s/latency) high => “Reliability risk / Dedicated candidate”
- Else scale => “Scale growth / Dedicated candidate”
Otherwise don’t route.

Avery Johnson: +1 on priority. Right now everything comes through as “PLG Signal: Intent” and it’s impossible to tell what’s real.

Stephanie Nguyen: Also: for accounts w/ an existing CSM owner, I’d rather CS be looped first (or at least tagged) vs AE creating new outreach. Can we add that as a suppression/route rule?

David Kim: We can check if `csm_owner` is set (or whatever property we use) and either (a) assign the task to CSM, or (b) create a task for AE but add CS as follower (HubSpot doesn’t have followers the same way, but we can create a second task or note). We should avoid doubling tasks.

Nikhil Sharma: Minimal version: if `csm_owner_id` present => route to CSM queue and create a note for AE owner only (no task). That avoids collisions.

Camila Reyes: Action items:
- Ben + Nikhil: propose updated intent definitions (2-event rule) by EOD tomorrow
- David: confirm feasible fields + read-guards (marketing touch + open task dedupe) + estimate
- Tyler: pick the “marketing touch” property name + workflow to stamp it
- Isabelle: draft the “task title/priority” mapping + share in thread
- Stephanie/Avery: keep a short list of the worst false positives (company names + what triggered) so we can validate suppression rules

Ben Carter: :thumbsup: I’ll also update the pilot dashboard to track “tasks/company” and “% suppressed” so we can see if changes are working.

Tyler Benson: One more thought: add an allowlist for the pilot (top N accounts by last-30d usage) until thresholds stabilize. That cuts blast radius.

Camila Reyes: I’m open to a temporary allowlist for NA week 2 if needed. Let’s try threshold + suppression first, but if we’re still >~40 tasks/week we should gate.

Isabelle Laurent: Here’s draft task mapping:
- Compliance: create task “PLG: Private/Compliance interest (review within 24h)”
- Reliability: “PLG: Reliability risk (429s/latency) — offer Dedicated / capacity review”
- Scale: “PLG: Scale growth — discuss Dedicated economics + throughput”
Include in body: signal summary, 7d metrics, links (docs viewed), and suppression reason if applicable.

David Kim: Noted. I’ll make sure the router writes a single consolidated note with:
- top_signal_family
- contributing_events
- window (7d/14d)
- recommended next step + playbook link

Avery Johnson: If we can get the “recommended next step” in the task, adoption will go way up.

Camila Reyes: Great. I’ll post an updated Week 2 plan in the pilot doc once we agree on exact cut lines. For now: please react :thumbsup: if you’re aligned with A/B/C/D above, or reply with edits.

title: #customer-success: 1746555302
preview:
Jasmine Liu: quick q as we roll PLG signals into HubSpot: when do we loop in CSMs, and how are we tagging the motion? specifically want a clean split between “existing customer expansion” vs “new self-serve conversion” so we don’t step on toes.

Rachel Kim: +1. for existing paid customers we need a heads-up before AE outreach. even if it’s “good news” (usage spike), it can be tied to a rollout that’s currently fragile.

Markus Klein: also: reliability signals (429s/latency) can be either “upgrade time” or “they’re hurting”. if AE jumps in with a pitch, it reads tone deaf.

Isabelle Laurent: from ops side: we can encode this as a tag/property on the PLG Signal object + company. question is the definition.

Ben Carter: PRD intent was: classify signals + score them, then route with guardrails. but we didn’t fully define CS notification rules. let’s lock a v1.

Jasmine Liu: proposal for v1 classification:
- “new_self_serve_conversion” = no current contract + no CSM assigned
- “existing_customer_expansion” = any paid status OR CSM assigned OR currently in CS book
- “cs_risk/support” (or “support-first”) = signals that indicate degraded experience (repeated 5xx, sustained 429s, error spikes) esp if they have open support tickets

Rachel Kim: I like that, but make “paid status” explicit: Hosted paid counts as existing customer, Dedicated counts, Private counts. (Some folks treat paid Hosted as “still PLG” but for CS it’s still a customer relationship.)

Stephanie Nguyen: jumping in (saw this thread via @Jasmine). sales POV: we’re aligned on looping CSM for existing customers. for the NA pilot we were thinking: task goes to AE, but task body includes “CSM: X” + a checkbox-y step: “sync with CSM before contacting.”

Jasmine Liu: +1, but I’d rather we gate outreach than rely on task template compliance. could we do “notify CSM” AND “delay AE task 24h” if existing customer expansion?

David Kim: technically doable. router can set:
- `plg_signal_motion` (enum)
- `plg_signal_requires_csm_ack` (boolean)
- `plg_signal_ae_task_delay_hours` (int)
HubSpot workflow can handle the delay OR router can set due date accordingly.

Isabelle Laurent: careful: HubSpot workflow delays can get messy + hard to audit. I’d prefer router sets the task due date + writes a timeline note that includes the classification + reason.

Markus Klein: also: “existing customer expansion” shouldn’t block SE involvement. sometimes it’s an expansion that needs fast technical help.

Rachel Kim: agreed. looping CSM doesn’t mean slow. it just means coordinated. if it’s an expansion, CSM can provide context (stakeholders, renewal date, current plan limits, support history).

Ben Carter: definitions—what’s the source of truth for “existing customer”? billing system has it, HubSpot may be stale. for v1 we can use:
1) billing status in data warehouse (paid subscription) if available in the daily job
2) else fallback to HubSpot property `customer_status`
3) else if CSM assigned property present -> treat as existing

Isabelle Laurent: +1 on hierarchy. also need a carve-out: former customer churned 12+ months ago shouldn’t automatically be “existing expansion.” (they might be net-new again.)

Jasmine Liu: can we define:
- if `customer_status in {Paid, Dedicated, Private}` => existing_customer_expansion
- if `customer_status = Churned` and `churn_date > 180d` => treat as new_self_serve_conversion
- if `csm_owner` present regardless => existing_customer_expansion

David Kim: we have churn date for most accounts, not all. but can implement “if churn date missing, default to existing if CSM assigned.”

Stephanie Nguyen: ok but what about accounts that have a CSM because they were in an enterprise trial/POC (not paying yet)?

Rachel Kim: if CS is engaged, we still need coordination. I’m fine tagging those as existing_customer_expansion (or “cs_engaged”) even if not technically “paid.”

Markus Klein: maybe rename to something broader like “cs_owned_expansion” vs “net-new conversion”? but in HubSpot we can keep the label “existing customer expansion” as long as logic includes “CS engaged”.

Jasmine Liu: works. in playbook language we can explain: “existing customer expansion = any account with an assigned CSM or active/past paid relationship within last 180 days.”

Ben Carter: what’s the notification mechanism to CS? just via HubSpot note/property, or Slack ping?

Jasmine Liu: v1 = no Slack spam. we should rely on a CS view/list in HubSpot + weekly review in CS standup. later we can add Slack pings only for high-severity/high-confidence.

Rachel Kim: agree. Slack pings will get ignored fast.

Isabelle Laurent: actionable rules proposal:
- If `plg_signal_motion = new_self_serve_conversion`: create AE task immediately (standard cadence)
- If `plg_signal_motion = existing_customer_expansion`: create AE task with due date +24h AND create a “CSM heads-up” task assigned to CSM (or add to CSM queue)
- If `plg_signal_motion = support_first`: no sales task; create CS/support task and set `sales_suppressed_reason = support_first`

Markus Klein: + add “support_first” if there’s an open P0/P1 incident tag or recent support ticket volume spike (if we have that signal). if we don’t have support data yet, we can approximate using product error signals.

David Kim: we don’t have Zendesk synced in this phase. but we can use telemetry: sustained 5xx + high 429 rate + recent latency regressions -> set `support_first = true`.

Stephanie Nguyen: for “existing customer expansion”, if there is already an AE owner (most will), we preserve it. but CS task helps prevent conflicting outreach. I’m good with 24h delay if that reduces mishaps.

Jasmine Liu: cool. also want a tag to differentiate expansion type:
- `expansion_driver = scale | compliance | team_rollout | performance`
so CSM can prep context for the AE.

Ben Carter: driver can map from signal type (docs intent vs threshold). should be doable.

Rachel Kim: and please add an explicit label for “team expansion” signals (new workspaces/org invites). those are perfect for CS-led rollout planning, not just AE pricing talk.

Isabelle Laurent: ok, so properties we’ll standardize (names tbd but semantics):
- `plg_signal_motion` = new_self_serve_conversion | existing_customer_expansion | support_first
- `plg_signal_motion_reason` = paid_status | csm_assigned | churned_recently | reliability_degradation
- `plg_signal_driver` = scale | compliance | reliability | team_expansion | pricing_intent

David Kim: I’ll align with the existing HubSpot property spec so we don’t proliferate fields.

Jasmine Liu: next steps:
1) I’ll update the CS handoff doc section w/ these definitions
2) @Isabelle/@David can you sanity check property names + what’s implementable in router vs workflows
3) @Stephanie can you reflect 24h delay for “existing customer expansion” in the AE pilot expectations (so no one thinks tasks are “late”)

Stephanie Nguyen: yup, will update the pilot notes + tell AEs “existing customer signals may show up w/ later due dates by design.”

Rachel Kim: :thumbsup: thanks. this should reduce the “surprise outreach” issues.

Markus Klein: :thumbsup: and pls make sure task template says: “check if this is support-first / verify customer experience before pitching upgrade.”

Ben Carter: :thumbsup: I’ll also add this to the playbook so it’s not tribal knowledge.

David Kim: :thumbsup: will post proposed property mapping in the HubSpot spec doc once I reconcile naming.

title: #eng-platform: 1746501123
preview:
Maria Chen: heads up — collector CPU climbed ~3x after yesterday's routing metadata rollout. p99 for the ingest worker jumped and OOMs started in a couple canaries. @alex can you take a look? :eyes:
Alex Patel: on it. which change specifically? the route-side enrichment you mentioned in the PR?
Maria Chen: yeah, the route metadata enrichment that appends 3-5 extra attributes per request. rolled to ~20% traffic yesterday at 09:05. saw CPU start spiking ~09:12.
sre-bot: alert: telemetry-collector.high_cpu detected on telemetry-prod-collector-3 (host tpc-03) — cpu 89% sustained, p99 loop latency ↑
Jia Li: grabbing pprof now, sending top of profile.
Jia Li: ```
(pprof) top10
Showing nodes accounting for 84.32% of 10s sampling
      flat  flat%   sum%        cum   cum%   name
     1.9s  19.00% 19.00%     3.2s  32.00%  runtime.mallocgc
     1.5s  15.00% 34.00%     2.6s  26.00%  telemetry/attributes.AppendTags
     1.2s  12.00% 46.00%     2.1s  21.00%  collector/ingest.ProcessBatch
     0.8s   8.00% 54.00%     1.1s  11.00%  encoding/json.Marshal
```
Jia Li: attributes.AppendTags is the obvious hotspot — costs proportional to number of extra attrs.
Ben Carter: did we change sampling or retention at the same time? could be additive.
Maria Chen: no sampling change. retention TTL same. only difference was adding route labels and the `route.version` tag for every span.
Leo G: rough mitigation options: 1) reduce enrichment for high-qps routes (drop optional tags), 2) increase collector batching window from 10ms -> 30ms to amortize cost, 3) move enrichment earlier to edge where we can sample/drop earlier.
Alex Patel: can we toggle enrichment behind a feature-flag per-route? that would let us canary-off heavy routes. Console rollout UX: need an easy per-route flag with immediate effect (no deploy).
Maria Chen: yes — created a feature-flag in config service, toggle path: `/flags/telemetry/route-enrich/<route-id>` — tested locally.
Jia Li: quick A/B numbers from a small run (synthetic traffic):
- enrichment=on: collector CPU 78%, p95 request-process 1.2s, p99 2.8s
- enrichment=off: collector CPU 32%, p95 0.4s, p99 0.9s
Alex Patel: that's a huge delta. We should also add a per-route span-size histogram and an attribute-count gauge to the Console dashboards so product owners can see which routes create the worst telemetry load.
Ben Carter: if we drop tags for some routes we may affect downstream customers relying on them. Need to coordinate on the Console and docs to list which tags are optional.
Maria Chen: agreed. Plan proposal: 1) back out enrichment for top-10 QPS routes via the flag (immediate), 2) bump collector batching window and run 50% canary, 3) add histograms and attr-count metrics to Console, 4) document optional tags and set 3-week deprecation notice if teams need them.
Leo G: also consider adding a cheap server-side sampler that drops 1-x spans earlier for very chatty routes — cheaper than full enrich.
Alex Patel: I'll wire the dashboard panels (span-size, attr-count, route-qps) and add a Console action to flip the route flag. Can someone own communications to SRE and product teams?
Maria Chen: I will send a note to #customer-success and product owners. Ben — can you own the docs?
Ben Carter: yep, I'll draft the doc and tag owners.
sre-bot: reminder: scheduled canary window for flag rollback 14:00 UTC
Maria Chen: thanks — rolling flags now for top-10. will report back with pprof after 10m.
Jia Li: noted, will re-run pprof in 10m and paste results here.
Alex Patel: long term follow-ups: prefer doing enrichment at the control-plane (cheap key lookups), add circuit-breaker for per-route enrichment, and expose estimated token/cpu cost per tag in the Console.
Maria Chen: +1. urgency: high (production p99 impact). low-risk immediate action: flag toggle. middle-term: batching + dashboard. longer-term: move enrichment and sampling earlier.
Jia Li: pprof 10m after flag toggle — cpu back to baseline ~34%, AppendTags heat gone.
Maria Chen: awesome, rolling plan to full rollback for 24h and schedule a follow-up RFC for staged enrichment UX. :tada:

title: #eng-security: 1746481120
preview:
Naomi Feldman: Dropping the latest draft of the regulated workloads Security FAQ here for claims review. This is the version Sales will send + we’ll link from the landing page. Please flag any risky language + what evidence we need to attach/link. (Drive export -> Confluence page later today)

Soojin Lee: thx @Naomi Feldman — key deltas since last pass: added SSO/SAML section, tightened retention language, added “audit logs” bullets. Still seeing some pushback from Marketing on removing “HIPAA-ready” so we need a clear recommendation.

Olga Petrov: I’m reading through now. Starting a running list of “approved vs don’t say” lines.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: +1, let’s keep this conservative. Regulated buyers will scrutinize every adjective.

Daniel Carter: If you call out evidence needed, I can map to what’s already in the evidence pack index (SOC2 request process, diagrams, sample logs) and add missing artifacts.

Naomi Feldman: Great. Please reply in-thread with exact line edits if possible.

Olga Petrov: Section: “Compliance posture”
Current line: “Redwood is SOC 2 certified and ISO 27001 compliant.”
- Change to: “Redwood maintains a SOC 2 Type II report (available under NDA). Redwood operates an ISO 27001-aligned security program.”
Reason: we shouldn’t say “ISO certified” unless we have the actual certificate + scope handy.
Evidence link needed: SOC2 request instructions + (if applicable) ISO cert + statement of applicability.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Agree. Also add scope qualifier.
Suggested add: “SOC 2 report covers Redwood Inference’s hosted service and corporate systems (details in report).”
Don’t imply it covers on-prem customer environments.

Naomi Feldman: Noted. We do have SOC2 Type II; ISO is “aligned” not certified today.

Soojin Lee: Copy question: can we still say “SOC2 Type II” explicitly? (Marketing wants it in the first 2 paragraphs.)

Olga Petrov: Yes, “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA” is fine.

Elliot Price: From docs perspective, I’ll need a stable URL to point to (internal evidence index page + external “request SOC2” flow). If we’re gating behind NDA, make that explicit.

Kira Thompson: +1. Also please avoid “certified” in general—Docs keeps getting called on it.

Olga Petrov: Section: “HIPAA / PHI”
Current line: “HIPAA-ready deployments for PHI workloads.”
- Recommend remove entirely. Replace with: “We support deployment patterns used by customers building PHI-adjacent workflows (e.g., VPC/private networking, encryption, access controls, audit logs). Customers are responsible for determining HIPAA applicability and executing a BAA where required.”
Reason: “HIPAA-ready” reads like a compliance claim.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Strong agree. Also do not say “HIPAA compliant.” If someone asks, the only safe answer is: we can support a BAA on Dedicated/Private subject to review.

Soojin Lee: Understood. Can we say “BAA available”? Or is that too strong?

Naomi Feldman: We can say: “BAA considered/available for Dedicated/Private with appropriate support tier” (we need Legal’s final word). Let’s avoid promising it in the general FAQ.

Ben Carter: (Marketing) ok, we’ll drop “HIPAA-ready.” We’ll use “PHI-adjacent” language + steer CTA to security review call.

Olga Petrov: Section: “Data usage / training”
Current line: “We never train on customer data.”
- Change to: “We do not use customer prompts or outputs to train Redwood models without explicit customer agreement.”
Reason: “never” + “customer data” is broad (logs/telemetry). Also if we use any data for abuse monitoring, must caveat.
Evidence: internal policy excerpt we can share + DPA language reference.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Yes, and add: “Data is processed solely to provide the service, maintain security, and meet contractual obligations.”

Naomi Feldman: Good catch. We also need to align with DPA wording.

Olga Petrov: Section: “Retention”
Current line: “Zero data retention by default.”
- This is risky. Replace with: “Configurable retention options are available. By default, Redwood retains limited service logs for a defined period for reliability and security monitoring. Retention periods can be reduced for Dedicated/Private subject to configuration and contract.”
Reason: “zero retention” invites a gotcha unless we can guarantee across all systems.
Evidence: retention policy statement + actual default durations (we need to confirm exact numbers!).

Kira Thompson: +1000. “Zero retention” also conflicts with audit/logging story.

Markus Klein: From SE side, we’ve been saying “we can do reduced retention + customer-managed log export” for Private. Agree w/ Olga. Also: if asked, we can describe “payload logging disabled” separately from “system logs.”

Naomi Feldman: Action item for me: confirm default durations + what’s actually configurable per product (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private). I’ll sync with Product.

Olga Petrov: Section: “Encryption”
Current line: “All data is encrypted at rest and in transit with customer-managed keys.”
- Split into two bullets:
  1) “Data in transit is protected using TLS.”
  2) “Data at rest is encrypted. For Private deployments, customer-managed key integrations (KMS/HSM) are available.”
Reason: “customer-managed keys” is not universally true (Hosted vs Private).
Evidence: encryption diagram + KMS integration doc snippet.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Also avoid saying HSM unless we can name supported integrations. Say “KMS/HSM integrations available” only if list is in evidence pack or docs.

Daniel Carter: We have an “encryption + key management” diagram in the evidence pack index (internal). I can add a short list of supported providers (AWS KMS, GCP KMS, Azure Key Vault) and note “HSM via customer KMS” wording.

Elliot Price: Please give me exact phrasing; docs links need to match to avoid drift.

Olga Petrov: Section: “Audit logging”
Current line: “Full audit logs for all actions.”
- Change to: “Audit logs capture key administrative and access events (e.g., user/role changes, auth events, key configuration changes, model deployment actions). Audit logs can be exported to customer systems in Dedicated/Private.”
Reason: “full” and “all actions” are absolute.
Evidence: sample audit event schema + list of event types.

Naomi Feldman: Yes, and we should align to what’s in the engineering ticket. @Daniel Carter can you link the sample payload we used with Brightline?

Daniel Carter: I can. There’s a sample audit log payload we can sanitize + include as “example only.” I’ll reference it in the evidence pack index and we can link from FAQ.

Olga Petrov: Section: “SSO / SAML / SCIM”
Current line: “SAML and SCIM supported.”
- Add qualifiers: “SSO via SAML 2.0 supported for Console access (Dedicated/Private). SCIM provisioning supported for enterprise plans.”
Reason: we’ve had edge cases + plan gating.
Evidence: SSO setup guide + known limitations note (metadata validation, required attributes).

Markus Klein: +1. We have an open/closed support ticket on metadata validation. Don’t promise “any IdP works out of the box.”

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Add “RBAC” detail too: “Role-based access controls available; permissions vary by role.” Keep it short.

Soojin Lee: Quick question: ok to say “supports data residency / regional isolation”? PMM wants it for EMEA.

Naomi Feldman: Only if we phrase carefully.
Suggested: “Regional deployment options available. For Private deployments, customers control the region/VPC where services run. For Hosted, region availability depends on Redwood’s cloud regions.”
Don’t say “guaranteed residency” unless contract + region specified.

Olga Petrov: +1. Avoid “data never leaves region” unless we can prove for all telemetry/support tooling.

Elliot Price: If we include residency, we need a footnote pointing to the “regional availability” page + “support access” statement.

Daniel Carter: Evidence pack currently has a short “data residency / support access” statement. I’ll verify it’s up to date.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Also: remove any mention of FedRAMP / PCI unless we actually have it. I saw “federal-ready” in one earlier draft.

Soojin Lee: Confirmed: no FedRAMP/PCI language in this latest version. (We had it in comments only; I’ll delete comment threads before publishing.)

Naomi Feldman: Thank you. Summary of changes we’ll make before this goes to Sales:
- Replace “ISO compliant/certified” with “ISO-aligned program” (unless we find cert)
- Remove “HIPAA-ready” and avoid “HIPAA compliant”; add PHI-adjacent + BAA caveat
- Replace “never train” + “zero retention” absolutes with qualified policy language
- Split encryption claims + scope customer-managed keys to Private
- Replace “full audit logs for all actions” with event-based wording + export qualifier
- Add plan/scope qualifiers to SAML/SCIM
- Tighten residency language + add footnotes

Ben Carter: What’s the SLA on getting this updated? We want landing page copy lock by tomorrow noon PT.

Olga Petrov: I can turn edits in ~2 hours once Soojin shares the exact doc link (not the PDF export). Need to ensure track-changes is captured.

Soojin Lee: I’ll push an updated draft in the next 30 mins and @mention you + Naomi.

Daniel Carter: I’ll update the evidence pack index with:
- SOC2 request instructions (NDA gating)
- encryption/key management diagram + supported KMS list
- sanitized audit log sample payload
- retention policy statement (pending Naomi confirming defaults)

Elliot Price: Once the language is finalized, I’ll add cross-links in the docs PR (regulated landing page -> security FAQ -> evidence pack index). Please keep the external FAQ consistent with the internal Confluence version.

Kira Thompson: Also please add a “Last reviewed by Security” line + date at bottom of the FAQ. Helps with sales reuse.

Naomi Feldman: Good call. I’ll add “Reviewed: Security (GRC) / Legal: as needed” + review cadence.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Final note: wherever we say “available,” add “subject to plan and contract” once. Otherwise Sales will screenshot the FAQ and treat it as a promise. :)

Naomi Feldman: :thumbsup: will do. I’ll post the revised version for a quick final scan later today.

Olga Petrov: I’m reading through now. Starting a running list of “approved vs don’t say” lines.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: +1, let’s keep this conservative. Regulated buyers will scrutinize every adjective.

Daniel Carter: If you call out evidence needed, I can map to what’s already in the evidence pack index (SOC2 request process, diagrams, sample logs) and add missing artifacts.

Naomi Feldman: Great. Please reply in-thread with exact line edits if possible.

Olga Petrov: Section: “Compliance posture”
Current line: “Redwood is SOC 2 certified and ISO 27001 compliant.”
- Change to: “Redwood maintains a SOC 2 Type II report (available under NDA). Redwood operates an ISO 27001-aligned security program.”
Reason: we shouldn’t say “ISO certified” unless we have the actual certificate + scope handy.
Evidence link needed: SOC2 request instructions + (if applicable) ISO cert + statement of applicability.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Agree. Also add scope qualifier.
Suggested add: “SOC 2 report covers Redwood Inference’s hosted service and corporate systems (details in report).”
Don’t imply it covers on-prem customer environments.

Naomi Feldman: Noted. We do have SOC2 Type II; ISO is “aligned” not certified today.

Soojin Lee: Copy question: can we still say “SOC2 Type II” explicitly? (Marketing wants it in the first 2 paragraphs.)

Olga Petrov: Yes, “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA” is fine.

Elliot Price: From docs perspective, I’ll need a stable URL to point to (internal evidence index page + external “request SOC2” flow). If we’re gating behind NDA, make that explicit.

Kira Thompson: +1. Also please avoid “certified” in general—Docs keeps getting called on it.

Olga Petrov: Section: “HIPAA / PHI”
Current line: “HIPAA-ready deployments for PHI workloads.”
- Recommend remove entirely. Replace with: “We support deployment patterns used by customers building PHI-adjacent workflows (e.g., VPC/private networking, encryption, access controls, audit logs). Customers are responsible for determining HIPAA applicability and executing a BAA where required.”
Reason: “HIPAA-ready” reads like a compliance claim.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Strong agree. Also do not say “HIPAA compliant.” If someone asks, the only safe answer is: we can support a BAA on Dedicated/Private subject to review.

Soojin Lee: Understood. Can we say “BAA available”? Or is that too strong?

Naomi Feldman: We can say: “BAA considered/available for Dedicated/Private with appropriate support tier” (we need Legal’s final word). Let’s avoid promising it in the general FAQ.

Ben Carter: (Marketing) ok, we’ll drop “HIPAA-ready.” We’ll use “PHI-adjacent” language + steer CTA to security review call.

Olga Petrov: Section: “Data usage / training”
Current line: “We never train on customer data.”
- Change to: “We do not use customer prompts or outputs to train Redwood models without explicit customer agreement.”
Reason: “never” + “customer data” is broad (logs/telemetry). Also if we use any data for abuse monitoring, must caveat.
Evidence: internal policy excerpt we can share + DPA language reference.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Yes, and add: “Data is processed solely to provide the service, maintain security, and meet contractual obligations.”

Naomi Feldman: Good catch. We also need to align with DPA wording.

Olga Petrov: Section: “Retention”
Current line: “Zero data retention by default.”
- This is risky. Replace with: “Configurable retention options are available. By default, Redwood retains limited service logs for a defined period for reliability and security monitoring. Retention periods can be reduced for Dedicated/Private subject to configuration and contract.”
Reason: “zero retention” invites a gotcha unless we can guarantee across all systems.
Evidence: retention policy statement + actual default durations (we need to confirm exact numbers!).

Kira Thompson: +1000. “Zero retention” also conflicts with audit/logging story.

Markus Klein: From SE side, we’ve been saying “we can do reduced retention + customer-managed log export” for Private. Agree w/ Olga. Also: if asked, we can describe “payload logging disabled” separately from “system logs.”

Naomi Feldman: Action item for me: confirm default durations + what’s actually configurable per product (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private). I’ll sync with Product.

Olga Petrov: Section: “Encryption”
Current line: “All data is encrypted at rest and in transit with customer-managed keys.”
- Split into two bullets:
  1) “Data in transit is protected using TLS.”
  2) “Data at rest is encrypted. For Private deployments, customer-managed key integrations (KMS/HSM) are available.”
Reason: “customer-managed keys” is not universally true (Hosted vs Private).
Evidence: encryption diagram + KMS integration doc snippet.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Also avoid saying HSM unless we can name supported integrations. Say “KMS/HSM integrations available” only if list is in evidence pack or docs.

Daniel Carter: We have an “encryption + key management” diagram in the evidence pack index (internal). I can add a short list of supported providers (AWS KMS, GCP KMS, Azure Key Vault) and note “HSM via customer KMS” wording.

Elliot Price: Please give me exact phrasing; docs links need to match to avoid drift.

Olga Petrov: Section: “Audit logging”
Current line: “Full audit logs for all actions.”
- Change to: “Audit logs capture key administrative and access events (e.g., user/role changes, auth events, key configuration changes, model deployment actions). Audit logs can be exported to customer systems in Dedicated/Private.”
Reason: “full” and “all actions” are absolute.
Evidence: sample audit event schema + list of event types.

Naomi Feldman: Yes, and we should align to what’s in the engineering ticket. @Daniel Carter can you link the sample payload we used with Brightline?

Daniel Carter: I can. There’s a sample audit log payload we can sanitize + include as “example only.” I’ll reference it in the evidence pack index and we can link from FAQ.

Olga Petrov: Section: “SSO / SAML / SCIM”
Current line: “SAML and SCIM supported.”
- Add qualifiers: “SSO via SAML 2.0 supported for Console access (Dedicated/Private). SCIM provisioning supported for enterprise plans.”
Reason: we’ve had edge cases + plan gating.

Olga Petrov: +1. Avoid “data never leaves region” unless we can prove for all telemetry/support tooling.

Elliot Price: If we include residency, we need a footnote pointing to the “regional availability” page + “support access” statement.

Daniel Carter: Evidence pack currently has a short “data residency / support access” statement. I’ll verify it’s up to date.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Also: remove any mention of FedRAMP / PCI unless we actually have it. I saw “federal-ready” in one earlier draft.

Soojin Lee: Confirmed: no FedRAMP/PCI language in this latest version. (We had it in comments only; I’ll delete comment threads before publishing.)

Naomi Feldman: Thank you. Summary of changes we’ll make before this goes to Sales:

- Remove “HIPAA-ready” and avoid “HIPAA compliant”; add PHI-adjacent + BAA caveat

Soojin Lee: I’ll push an updated draft in the next 30 mins and @mention you + Naomi.

Kira Thompson: Heads up: opened docs PR for the Regulated Workloads landing page + cross-links. Would love eyes today/tomorrow so we can ship before the webinar promo. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood-docs/pull/421

Kira Thompson: Review asks:
- PMM: headline/value prop + “approved language” check
- Security: claims + links to Security FAQ / evidence pack index
- Docs: final checklist (nav, redirects, SEO, broken links)

Kira Thompson: Planned path: `/solutions/regulated-workloads/` (matches other solution pages). In nav: Solutions > Regulated workloads (new). Also added a footer link from Security page.

Kira Thompson: Yep, I used “PHI-adjacent” and “regulated workloads” only. No HIPAA or PCI claims.

Naomi Feldman: Looping in. I’m mainly looking for:
1) No over-claims about SOC2/ISO (must be precise)
2) KMS/HSM phrasing (don’t imply we run inside customer HSM)
3) Evidence pack access control (should be “available under NDA/request”)

Ben Carter: Thanks. For CTA we want: primary = “Request a security review” (routes to form), secondary = “Talk to an engineer” or “Get an architecture review”. Can we keep “Download evidence pack” off the public page?

Elliot Price: +1 to not offering a direct evidence pack download. We can link to a request page or a short “what’s included” page.

Daniel Carter: Evidence pack index exists (internal) and we have a sanitized “index” we can share, but it still shouldn’t be publicly discoverable. Recommend copy: “Evidence pack available upon request” with a contact CTA.

Kira Thompson: Current PR has:
- “Security FAQ for regulated workloads” link (public docs page)
- “Evidence pack (available upon request)” link -> takes you to a short page that explains contents and points to request form
No direct SOC2 link.

Naomi Feldman: Good. Where does “Security FAQ” land? If it’s the sales-ready Confluence doc, we can’t link that publicly.

Kira Thompson: Not Confluence. I created a docs page under `/security/regulated-workloads-faq/` with the approved Q&A (sanitized) + “contact security” at the bottom. It intentionally doesn’t include report artifacts.

Soojin Lee: Reading now. One nit: section header “Compliance & Certifications” reads like we’re listing a bunch. Suggest “Security posture” and keep it to a few bullets + “details available under NDA.”

Kira Thompson: Makes sense. I’ll rename header and tighten.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Skimmed the page. Overall direction is fine. A few wording tweaks:
- Replace “SOC 2 certified” with “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA” (we never say “certified”)
- “Data residency” should be “deployment region and data locality controls” unless we’re explicitly committing to residency guarantees
- For encryption: “in transit and at rest” is ok, but don’t say “end-to-end”

Kira Thompson: Got it. Will update those exact phrases.

Elliot Price: Docs checklist items I’m watching:
- Add to sitemap + set canonical URL
- Meta title/description (SEO)
- No orphaned pages; ensure nav and internal links are consistent
- If we’re adding `/solutions/regulated-workloads/`, we should add redirect from any prior draft path (if it existed)

Kira Thompson: Added canonical + meta, but will double-check. There was a draft at `/solutions/regulated/` in a branch a while ago (never shipped). No redirect in prod.

Tyler Benson: For the CTA form links, please add UTMs consistent w/ the ABM pilot:
`utm_source=docs&utm_medium=owned&utm_campaign=abm_regulated&utm_content=regulated_landing`
Also for the “Request a security review” button, can we tag `utm_term=security_review`?

Kira Thompson: Yep, I’ll add UTMs across both CTAs.

Grace O'Connor: Quick SDR workflow question: where do inbound requests land? We need them to hit the “Regulated ABM” routing rule in HubSpot (so we don’t lose these).

Ben Carter: Good call. Tyler + I have a routing rule already for `abm_regulated` campaign. As long as UTMs are set, we’re good.

Naomi Feldman: One more: on the FAQ page, in the “Audit logs” answer, please avoid listing specific field names unless we’re 100% sure they won’t change. Better: “request/response metadata, access events, admin actions” and then “sample schema available in evidence pack.”

Kira Thompson: I did list a few fields (request_id, model, timestamp). I’ll generalize and point to sample schema via request.

Soojin Lee: Copy suggestion: replace “predictable throughput” with “predictable performance under load (reserved capacity options)” in the hero. Makes it less jargon-y.

Kira Thompson: Updated.

GitHub: @redwood-docs-bot CI checks passed for PR #421 (build ok, lint ok, linkcheck ok).

Elliot Price: I left inline comments on: nav label capitalization, two broken anchors (minor), and the canonical tag. Otherwise looks ready once Security edits are applied.

Kira Thompson: Pushed fixes for anchors + header renames + SOC2 wording + audit log answer. PTAL.

Naomi Feldman: Reviewed latest commit. Looks good now. Approved from Security side as long as we keep the evidence pack link as “request access” (not a public download). :thumbsup:

Camila Reyes: Great. Ben/Kira let’s ship today if reviews are green. Also: can you add a tiny note under CTA: “We can share compliance documentation under NDA” (keeps expectations set).

Kira Thompson: Added the note (short + non-committal) and requested final approval from Elliot + Soojin.

Soojin Lee: Approved. Thanks for incorporating the language guardrails.

Elliot Price: Approved on my side. Merge when ready.

Kira Thompson: Merging now + will monitor deploy. Thanks all.

title: #general: 1746235510
preview:
Ben Carter: Heads up — locking speakers for the regulated workloads webinar. Targeting **Thu May 29** (45 min live + 15 min buffer). Need: (1) opening framing, (2) product/demo (Dedicated/Private), (3) security/compliance section + Q&A. Who’s available?

Ben Carter: Also quick check: any messaging landmines we need to run by Legal/Security *before* we start promoting? (Want to avoid another “HIPAA-ready” debate in public copy.)

Camila Reyes: I can join for open/close but would rather not be the primary speaker on the technical bits. If Aisha can do the credibility opener that’d be ideal.

Soojin Lee: +1 on messaging sensitivity. Please no “HIPAA compliant” / “guaranteed compliance” language. We can say “supports deployments with HIPAA-aligned controls” (and always pair with “customer config + policies apply”).

Soojin Lee: For promo copy we should stick to: audit logs, SSO/SAML, retention controls, KMS/HSM integrations, VPC/on-prem options. Avoid naming frameworks we don’t explicitly certify.

Markus Klein: I can do the demo portion. Prefer 10–12 min max. I’ll show Console: routing/fallback, per-route latency/cost, and what Dedicated vs Private looks like at a high level.

Markus Klein: Availability: May 29 works. If it slips to May 30, also ok. Week of June 2 I’m travel-heavy.

Fatima Noor: I can cover the reference architecture section (VPC + audit log export + KMS hooks) but I’m OOO May 29 afternoon PT. If you move to morning PT / early ET I can make it.

Simran Kaur: I can cover architecture and/or do the “what a 2-week technical pilot looks like” slide. May 29 is clear for me. If we want a pre-recorded demo, I can help cut it.

Naomi Feldman: Security section: I can do 8–10 min on posture + how we handle evidence requests (SOC 2 process, encryption, audit logs, retention/residency). Strong preference that we **do not** promise specific retention durations on the webinar (varies by product + contract). We can explain options.

Naomi Feldman: Also please keep “evidence pack” behind a request workflow (NDA where needed). We can point to the index and say “available under NDA.”

Dr. Aisha Rahman: I can do a brief opener (3–4 min) framing what regulated teams care about (auditability, least-privilege, isolation, provable controls) + what to ask vendors. May 29 works if we keep it tight.

Ben Carter: Amazing. Tentative agenda:
1) Aisha opener (4)
2) Soojin positioning + what’s in/out (5)
3) Markus demo (12)
4) Simran architecture + pilot plan (10)
5) Naomi security FAQ highlights + evidence pack process (10)
6) Q&A (10)

Ben Carter: Does that feel too long? We can trim.

Elliot Price: For messaging landmines: yes, please route the final abstract + landing page copy through Docs/Security review. I can do a quick editorial pass but I need the “approved claims” list from PMM (Soojin) so we don’t drift.

Elliot Price: Also: no product roadmap promises live. Keep it to current capabilities + near-term “we’re working on it” with no dates.

Michael Grant: Legal note: if we’re publishing a landing page + webinar description, I need **48 business hours** to review any claims around compliance, residency, retention, and SLAs. If you want to reference “HIPAA” at all, even as “HIPAA-aligned,” we should add a disclaimer slide + website footer language.

Michael Grant: Please avoid phrases like “certified,” “guarantees,” “meets all requirements,” etc. Use “can support,” “configurable,” “available under contract,” and keep it factual.

Tyler Benson: For scheduling: if we do May 29, best attendance historically is **11am PT / 2pm ET**. If Fatima needs morning PT, we could do **10am PT / 1pm ET**.

Grace O'Connor: From SDR side: better if we have a clear CTA in promo. “Book a security review” tends to get more qualified responses than “watch demo.” But we should confirm with Sales.

Camila Reyes: Agree w/ Grace. CTA should be “Get the security FAQ + talk to our solutions team” or “Schedule a security + architecture review.”

Soojin Lee: Re agenda length: trim my section to 3 min (what Redwood is + why Dedicated/Private for regulated). Or I can do a 60-sec “rules of the road” (what we can/can’t claim).

Markus Klein: I’ll keep demo to 8–10 min. Happy to skip anything that requires too much setup. I’ll show a pre-staged dashboard.

Naomi Feldman: Please include one explicit slide: “This webinar is informational and not compliance or legal advice.” (We can keep it simple.)

Ben Carter: Got it. Next steps:
- I’ll send calendar hold for **May 29 10am PT** (tentative) and we’ll confirm by EOD tomorrow.
- Soojin + Naomi: can you drop the “approved language” for the promo blurb here?
- Michael: I’ll route final copy by Monday morning for your 48h review.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Works. Please send me the exact opening prompt you want (1–2 bullets) so I don’t wander.

Soojin Lee: Promo blurb draft (approved-ish): “Deploy LLM inference for regulated workloads with predictable latency/cost and controls like audit logging, SSO/SAML, and private networking (VPC/on‑prem). Join Redwood Inference for a practical walkthrough of architectures, security considerations, and what to expect in a short technical pilot.”

Naomi Feldman: Add: “Evidence available under NDA” and avoid “PHI” unless we caveat “PHI-adjacent” and “customer determines data classification.”

Michael Grant: +1. Also remove “controls like” if it implies a definitive list. Better: “features that can support controls such as…”

Ben Carter: Noted. I’ll revise and share final copy in the Drive doc + tag for review. Thanks all.

(reaction: :thumbsup: from Camila Reyes)
(reaction: :white_check_mark: from Markus Klein)
text:
Ben Carter: Heads up — locking speakers for the regulated workloads webinar. Targeting **Thu May 29** (45 min live + 15 min buffer). Need: (1) opening framing, (2) product/demo (Dedicated/Private), (3) security/compliance section + Q&A. Who’s available?

Ben Carter: Also quick check: any messaging landmines we need to run by Legal/Security *before* we start promoting? (Want to avoid another “HIPAA-ready” debate in public copy.)

Camila Reyes: I can join for open/close but would rather not be the primary speaker on the technical bits. If Aisha can do the credibility opener that’d be ideal.

Soojin Lee: +1 on messaging sensitivity. Please no “HIPAA compliant” / “guaranteed compliance” language. We can say “supports deployments with HIPAA-aligned controls” (and always pair with “customer config + policies apply”).

Soojin Lee: For promo copy we should stick to: audit logs, SSO/SAML, retention controls, KMS/HSM integrations, VPC/on-prem options. Avoid naming frameworks we don’t explicitly certify.

Markus Klein: I can do the demo portion. Prefer 10–12 min max. I’ll show Console: routing/fallback, per-route latency/cost, and what Dedicated vs Private looks like at a high level.

Markus Klein: Availability: May 29 works. If it slips to May 30, also ok. Week of June 2 I’m travel-heavy.

Fatima Noor: I can cover the reference architecture section (VPC + audit log export + KMS hooks) but I’m OOO May 29 afternoon PT. If you move to morning PT / early ET I can make it.

Simran Kaur: I can cover architecture and/or do the “what a 2-week technical pilot looks like” slide. May 29 is clear for me. If we want a pre-recorded demo, I can help cut it.

Naomi Feldman: Security section: I can do 8–10 min on posture + how we handle evidence requests (SOC 2 process, encryption, audit logs, retention/residency). Strong preference that we **do not** promise specific retention durations on the webinar (varies by product + contract). We can explain options.

Naomi Feldman: Also please keep “evidence pack” behind a request workflow (NDA where needed). We can point to the index and say “available under NDA.”

Dr. Aisha Rahman: I can do a brief opener (3–4 min) framing what regulated teams care about (auditability, least-privilege, isolation, provable controls) + what to ask vendors. May 29 works if we keep it tight.

Ben Carter: Amazing. Tentative agenda:
1) Aisha opener (4)
2) Soojin positioning + what’s in/out (5)
3) Markus demo (12)
4) Simran architecture + pilot plan (10)
5) Naomi security FAQ highlights + evidence pack process (10)
6) Q&A (10)

Ben Carter: Does that feel too long? We can trim.

Elliot Price: For messaging landmines: yes, please route the final abstract + landing page copy through Docs/Security review. I can do a quick editorial pass but I need the “approved claims” list from PMM (Soojin) so we don’t drift.

Elliot Price: Also: no product roadmap promises live. Keep it to current capabilities + near-term “we’re working on it” with no dates.

Michael Grant: Legal note: if we’re publishing a landing page + webinar description, I need **48 business hours** to review any claims around compliance, residency, retention, and SLAs. If you want to reference “HIPAA” at all, even as “HIPAA-aligned,” we should add a disclaimer slide + website footer language.

Michael Grant: Please avoid phrases like “certified,” “guarantees,” “meets all requirements,” etc. Use “can support,” “configurable,” “available under contract,” and keep it factual.

Tyler Benson: For scheduling: if we do May 29, best attendance historically is **11am PT / 2pm ET**. If Fatima needs morning PT, we could do **10am PT / 1pm ET**.

Grace O'Connor: From SDR side: better if we have a clear CTA in promo. “Book a security review” tends to get more qualified responses than “watch demo.” But we should confirm with Sales.

Camila Reyes: Agree w/ Grace. CTA should be “Get the security FAQ + talk to our solutions team” or “Schedule a security + architecture review.”

Soojin Lee: Re agenda length: trim my section to 3 min (what Redwood is + why Dedicated/Private for regulated). Or I can do a 60-sec “rules of the road” (what we can/can’t claim).

Markus Klein: I’ll keep demo to 8–10 min. Happy to skip anything that requires too much setup. I’ll show a pre-staged dashboard.

Naomi Feldman: Please include one explicit slide: “This webinar is informational and not compliance or legal advice.” (We can keep it simple.)

Ben Carter: Got it. Next steps:
- I’ll send calendar hold for **May 29 10am PT** (tentative) and we’ll confirm by EOD tomorrow.
- Soojin + Naomi: can you drop the “approved language” for the promo blurb here?
- Michael: I’ll route final copy by Monday morning for your 48h review.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Works. Please send me the exact opening prompt you want (1–2 bullets) so I don’t wander.

Soojin Lee: Promo blurb draft (approved-ish): “Deploy LLM inference for regulated workloads with predictable latency/cost and controls like audit logging, SSO/SAML, and private networking (VPC/on‑prem). Join Redwood Inference for a practical walkthrough of architectures, security considerations, and what to expect in a short technical pilot.”

Michael Grant: +1. Also remove “controls like” if it implies a definitive list. Better: “features that can support controls such as…”

Ben Carter: Noted. I’ll revise and share final copy in the Drive doc + tag for review. Thanks all.

title: #marketing: 1746134552
preview:
Camila Reyes: seeing a few early pilot accounts get *both* lifecycle nurture + AE outreach in the same 24h :grimacing: we need suppression rules before this scales. can we align today on what gets suppressed and for how long?

Tyler Benson: +1. we had a Quartz-ish complaint last week (not the same acct but same vibe). in HubSpot we can suppress marketing sends when a company enters a “Sales outreach in progress” state, but we need the rules to be explicit.

Ben Carter: yup. from Growth side, nurture is doing its job (docs/pricing/security views) but when the signal router fires and Sales jumps in, we can’t look uncoordinated. what are we optimizing for? (meeting booked rate + no spam)

Stephanie Nguyen: for the high-intent signals we want AE/SDR touch to win. if marketing hits them with a generic “here’s our dedicated guide” email after I’ve already sent something, it looks automated and cheap.

Grace O'Connor: agree. also from SDR cadence perspective: if they’re in an active sequence, marketing nurture can hurt deliverability and confuse threading.

Camila Reyes: ok, proposal for discussion:
1) If AE/SDR task is created from PLG signal → suppress non-transactional marketing emails to contacts at that company for X days.
2) If marketing email sent in last Y hours → delay SDR task creation by 1 biz day (or at least add a note so SDR doesn’t double tap).
3) Exceptions list (billing/receipt emails, product incident comms, etc.)
Thoughts on X/Y?

Isabelle Laurent: from ops/hygiene: we need something deterministic + auditable. otherwise we’ll get “why didn’t I get that nurture” questions forever. also: suppression should be at *company* level (domain) not just a single contact.

Tyler Benson: technically easiest is company property set by the router, e.g. `plg_sales_outreach_suppressed_until` + `plg_sales_outreach_owner`.
Then marketing workflows check “now < suppressed_until” and skip/unenroll from specific enrollments.

David Kim: we can do this with a custom company property + a workflow that unenrolls from selected marketing workflows. we need to list which workflows count as “nurture” vs “transactional”. also note: unenrollment is one-way unless we explicitly re-enroll later.

Ben Carter: I’m +1 on “AE task created → marketing suppression”. I’m less sure about “marketing last 48h → delay AE task” because for big signals we want speed.
Maybe: no delay, but tag the task w/ “Marketing touched in last 48h: {campaign}” so AE can reference it.

Stephanie Nguyen: yes please do NOT delay AE tasks on high score signals. speed matters.
But add a guardrail like “don’t send the generic dedicated upsell email if Sales is assigned.”

Grace O'Connor: also can we set a cap like “max 1 outbound touch per day per company” across both? even a loose one would help.

Tyler Benson: cross-team cap is hard unless we centralize in a sequencing tool. but we can approximate with:
- Marketing suppression when Sales is in active outreach
- Sales gets a note/visibility into recent marketing sends

Jasmine Liu: adding CS note: for existing customers, CSM should be aware before we suppress lifecycle comms. some of those emails are product education that reduces support load. can we scope suppression to only “upgrade prompts” vs all nurture?

Camila Reyes: good point. we should suppress only “upgrade / dedicated/private prompts” + “pricing oriented” sends, not general product tips or release notes.

David Kim: doable if we define the list. we can tag marketing workflows with an internal category (UpgradeNurture vs ProductEducation vs Transactional). then suppression only applies to UpgradeNurture.

Isabelle Laurent: re: durations. suggestion:
- Suppress UpgradeNurture for 14 days after Sales task creation OR until Sales marks outcome.
- If outcome = disqualified/false positive → suppression expires immediately (or 24h).
- If outcome = meeting booked/op created → suppression extends to 30 days (Sales-led motion).

Ben Carter: I like outcome-based. keeps us from permanently removing them from lifecycle.

Stephanie Nguyen: 14 days is fine. for meeting booked, 30 days makes sense (we’ll do our own sequencing + SE followups).

Grace O'Connor: can we add another rule: if contact replies “not interested” / unsubscribe, that should update suppression + stop both sides. just making sure we don’t keep poking.

Tyler Benson: that’s already standard unsubscribe handling for marketing, but Sales tasks won’t automatically stop. we can add a “do_not_outreach_plg” flag on company that Sales workflows respect.

Camila Reyes: ok summarizing proposed rules (v0):
- When PLG router creates Sales task on a company → set `plg_sales_outreach_suppressed_until = now + 14d`.
- Marketing suppresses ONLY workflows tagged UpgradeNurture while suppressed_until active.
- Router adds to HubSpot task note: “Recent marketing touches (last 7d)” (if available) so SDR/AE can avoid doubling.
- If Sales logs outcome:
  - Disqualified / false positive → set suppressed_until = now + 1d
  - Meeting booked / opp created → set suppressed_until = now + 30d
- Transactional + ProductEducation emails are not suppressed.
Any objections before we implement?

David Kim: implementation note: we can’t easily pull “recent marketing touches” into the task unless we query marketing events, but we can at least link to the company timeline view filtered to “Marketing email”.

Tyler Benson: +1. link is fine for MVP.

Ben Carter: can we also get a shared calendar of upcoming UpgradeNurture sends? even if suppression works, it’d help planning.

Camila Reyes: yes. request: Tyler can you set up a shared “GTM Touches” calendar (or a doc) that lists:
- upgrade nurture campaigns + send dates
- product launch emails
- known SDR blitz weeks
We’ll keep it lightweight but visible.

Tyler Benson: I can do a Google Calendar + a running sheet in the GTM drive. we can drop links in #sales and #marketing.

Stephanie Nguyen: please include the pilot cohort list so we don’t accidentally blast the same accounts.

Isabelle Laurent: I’ll add a section for suppression rules + ownership in the HubSpot spec doc too.

Camila Reyes: perfect. David/Tyler: can you confirm ETA for the suppression workflow changes?

David Kim: if we keep it to 1 company property + workflow suppression for tagged UpgradeNurture, I can have it in staging tomorrow, prod by EOW (pending quick review).

Tyler Benson: I’ll share the calendar + sheet by EOD today.

Camila Reyes: great. I’ll post a short PSA once it’s live + where people should report conflicts.
